1FerCpjUuTWhCSbooRZMYb1WQ5yddkwjNU_aRJis.title: Sprint Planning
1FerCpjUuTWhCSbooRZMYb1WQ5yddkwjNU_aRJis.summary:
Deployment Status and Duplication
The 'okay capture' workflow was successfully deployed to production, but an ongoing issue regarding duplicated orders in Impetus and LifeFile requires further investigation into logs and databases. Separately, the script for creating PracticeSync accounts is being prepared for production deployment after successful staging implementation.

Automation Review and Migration Strategy
A plan is needed to fix existing automation issues and manage future automation work, necessitating an audit of existing workflows created by a prior developer. The consensus was to consult with Philip regarding which WooCommerce automations should be immediately migrated to Medusa and defer non-critical migrations until requirements are solidified.

Jhon Ray and Data Migration Updates
Jhon Ray reported the tracking status filter is near completion and is testing a hotfix for a bulk medication import issue affecting IV Cure medications. Data migration is focusing on finishing patient migration before moving on to products, while end-to-end testing remains blocked awaiting necessary Webugol page completion and plugin implementation
decisions:
Gregory Executes PracticeSync Account Script
Gregory executes PracticeSync production account creation script Nadjib prepares. This action handles the absence of Danylo by using Gregory's database access.

Nadjib Inquires Dosage Instruction Source
Nadjib asks Konrad source of dosage instructions to correct TPH issue. This information unblocks dosage correction task.

Farhan Migrates Workflows To Medusa
Farhan begins migrating necessary workflows to Medusa from WooCommerce. Farhan also reviews existing N8N workflows for fixes.

Define Medusa Automation Migration Scope
Nadjib, Philip define Medusa migration scope. Automations prioritize obvious workflows now, defer complex work until project finish.

IV Cure Bulk Drug Upload Via Backend
Backend bulk upload used for IV Cure drugs. This temporary measure avoids blocking IV Cure go-live while front-end bulk import fix is prioritized.
nextSteps: Nadjib Boumekhiet will ask Konrad about the dosage instructions issue and provide a plan for handling the automation work next week.
Gregory Popov will prepare the script for patient migration, check in with the speaker named Guja to review some hacks, and aim to complete the patient migration today.
Nadjib Boumekhiet will continue investigating the issue regarding duplicated orders in impetus.
Nadjib Boumekhiet will send the script for creating a PracticeSync account in production to Gregory Popov for execution.
Nadjib Boumekhiet will discuss the necessary Medusa workflows with Philip to identify which ones are needed.
Rochelle Blaza will call Jhon Ray Angcon after the meeting to discuss (1) adding new fields in the drug database, and (2) Gregory Popov's plan for pharmacy API training.
Gregory Popov will start migrating the products after the patient migration is completed.
Gregory Popov will call the speaker named Johan to quickly discuss what he is asking for.
Nadjib Boumekhiet will review all the workflows that Farhan created and fix the problems and issues in them.

1iklHyzMsiV4R_v56b5zxgeL6EfFJkuLEb-FrtCM.title: Thrive Rx Weekly Leadership Meeting
1iklHyzMsiV4R_v56b5zxgeL6EfFJkuLEb-FrtCM.summary:
Clean Room Delay and Strategy
Construction of the Thrive clean room is delayed as Allied has not yet provided an ETA for completion, prompting the decision to apply for a retail pharmacy license immediately to expedite opening.

Fulfillment Center Operation Plan
Thrive will operate temporarily as a fulfillment center by white labeling products compounded at Kadus, allowing Thrive to begin generating revenue, with a target operational date of April 1st.

Staffing and Technology Review
A former employee was terminated due to concerns over inventory handling, and while DRX is being implemented for Thrive, the team agreed to review the alternative 503A Success system
decisions:
Samantha Join Allied Health Calls
Samantha will join future calls with Chris and Allied Health representatives. Inclusion ensures team awareness regarding clean room construction status.

Start Thrive Fulfillment Center Operations
Thrive will operate as a fulfillment center using Kadus white labeling services until clean room construction finishes. Fulfillment center allows operations continuation and generation of revenue.

Use DRX System For Thrive
Thrive will use the DRX system for prescription processing integration with Pioneer. Impetus system remains the client-facing front end.

Move DRX License, Computers To Thrive
DRX license and necessary computer equipment will move to the Thrive site location. Samantha offered available computers for investment.

Target April 1st Fulfillment Launch
Target launch date for Thrive fulfillment center operations is April 1st. March will be utilized for buildout and personnel hiring.

File Retail Pharmacy License Application Now
Apply for a Retail Pharmacy license next week and use a change of service upgrade to 503A later when the clean room is complete. Simultaneously start Legit Script and DRX setup processes.

Distribute 503b Products Through Thrive
Thrive will distribute 503b products, specifically GLP1s and NAD+, using the fulfillment center model. Distribution depends on obtaining favorable pricing.
nextSteps: Jasmeet Singh will move the DRX license to Thrive and purchase the computers for the location.
Jasmeet Singh will include Samantha D'Agostino in the conversations with Chris and Allied regarding the clean room development.
Jasmeet Singh will apply for a retail pharmacy license for Thrive soon.
Samantha D'Agostino will check on the payment status for the $86 invoice.

Jasmeet Singh will send an email to Samantha D'Agostino about the 503A success system, and Samantha D'Agostino will look up the system and meet with the team to ensure understanding of the requirements.
The group will meet next week to discuss the logistics of the fulfillment center setup.

Campaign Migration Nearing Completion
The team has completed the majority of simple text campaign migrations, with the remaining 10 to 20 campaigns expected to be finalized soon. Following this, the focus will shift to integrating the Medusa platform with existing N8N workflows to support the upcoming e-commerce MVP launch.

WooCommerce Migration Deadline Set
A critical deadline was established for the WooCommerce migration to be live by mid-March to support the e-commerce MVP. The launch MVP will utilize a manual order fulfillment process involving HubSpot and ImpetusRX until full system integration is achieved.

Production Deployment Set Monday
Key preparations for the MVP include ensuring the ImpetusRX drug database is ready with SKUs and implementing Business Unit Admin capabilities. The team aims for a production deployment on Monday, pending configuration completion and coordination for patient data transfers from ScriptSure.
decisions:
Medusa Connection Workflow Identification
Farhan will prepare list of workflows currently connected via WordPress/Zapier that need Medusa integration. This list identifies Medusa connection points for N8N workflows.

MVP Launch Automation Scope
Discuss required automations with Nadjib and Philip for new e-commerce MVP launch. Group determines which automations make sense for initial launch.

WooCommerce Migration Deadline Set
WooCommerce migration must be live by mid-March. Nadjib ensures migration completes within two weeks maximum to meet deadline.

Exclude Rejection Approval Logic
Rejection and approval logic excluded from MVP launch scope for new e-commerce platform. Orders assumed approved before processing in ImpetusRX.

Delay Email Workflow Production Deployment
Production deployment of email workflow delayed until next week. Aim for Monday deployment, pending Danylo availability and commitment for production support.

Request Scripture Transfer Patient API Use
Rizzi will follow up with ScriptSure support team (DAW) requesting they use update patient API to transfer patients to Rosanna. Internal UI buttons not functioning requires development bandwidth not currently available.

Medication Formulation PDF Workaround
Quickest workaround for missing medication formulation in prescription PDF is copying formulation name into drug name field. This temporary measure implemented while awaiting backend fix.

1YoOB4Z98NKQ_phnfqinGkpK5906ZjI4ITtF6P1d.summary:
Pharmacy API Updates and Testing
The Pharmacy API is merged to staging, and LifeFile integrations are confirmed with several pharmacies, while the Dispense Pro integration is in the testing phase requiring configuration support.

Infrastructure Access and Workflows
Discussion centered on securing Kubernetes credentials, as access is needed for infrastructure tasks, and the team agreed to attempt retrieval from the current owner for secure storage and distribution. A critical issue with TPH missing tracking information was resolved, and workflows for other projects were noted as overdue.

E-commerce Migration and Customizations
Plans were made for the e-commerce migration, focusing on integrating Medusa with N8N to replace the current Zapier/WordPress setup and establishing business unit-specific features, such as searching by SKU in the pharmacy marketplace view for Valhalla Vitality users.
decisions:
IV Cure Email Flow Production Deployment
IV Cure prescription email flow tests complete today in staging environment. Flow deploys to production before other Pharmacy API features.

IV Cure Testing Email Configuration
IV Cure prescription email integration configuration sends testing emails to team address first. Team then switches configuration to live production email.

Medusa E-commerce N8N Integration Research
Nadjib researches integration method for Medusa e-commerce platform with N8N workflows. Connection uses REST API since N8N lacks built-in Medusa integration.

Obsolete Webhook Submission Ticket Closure
Ticket for 'Web hook submission on Vality' closes. Ticket content obsolete and no longer needed.

Valhalla Vitality OK Capsule Integration
Greg implements plugin or solution for Valhalla Vitality e-commerce (Medusa) to manage OK Capsule order tracking. Ironsail Pharma, OK Capsule integration postponed since no current request exists.
nextSteps: Add the Kubernetes credentials to LastPass for secure distribution once Gregory receives them from Danylo.
Assist Elguja today with the configuration of Dispense Pro integrations so it can be tested in the staging environment.
Reach out to Danylo to obtain credentials for Kubernetes access.
Create a ticket for Danylo today regarding permissions, developer lead access, and infrastructure controls.
Investigate the customization of the pharmacy marketplace view for Valhalla Vitality, specifically allowing users to search for drugs by SKU.
Catch up with the team on the Peaks Creation project and outline the necessary flow.
Meet with the Webugol team to determine which parts of the e-commerce migration he will be responsible for.
Work with Gregory today to get Dispense Pro ready for testing by Rochelle and Rizzi.
Find time today to address the email from Michael Franks and Ethan regarding True Life Meds testing.
Check the date format tracking PR to ensure it is correctly merged and deployed, as it currently shows YYYYMMDD instead of MMDD.
Review Farhan’s work on the N8N workflows and provide Philip with an assessment and a path forward.
Send N8N credentials to Elguja and Gregory so they can review the current automations.
Begin researching how to connect Medusa to N8N workflows via REST API for the e-commerce migration.
Follow up with Philip to confirm if DiRX can accept controlled substances.
Continue coordinating tasks for the data migration of patient and prescription history to ImpetusRX.

1zRRHfdXUTyeztgRrT-fYOErxlSSj7fvFjdpe7dD.title: Sprint Planning
1zRRHfdXUTyeztgRrT-fYOErxlSSj7fvFjdpe7dD.summary:
Technical Issues Resolved
The team resolved critical technical issues, including a web hook tracking failure caused by Cloudflare's browser integrity check and a related logging speed problem, ensuring better data flow and faster log searches moving forward.

Backend and Tracking Fixes
Development is progressing on the Medusa backend deployment for Vitality MSO and addressing critical RXC tracking and prescription delivery issues in the V2 system, which requires immediate production deployment.

Valhalla Vitality MVP Defined
The main focus is defining the Valhalla Vitality migration MVP, prioritizing a quick patient log-in and purchasing solution on Medusa without immediate Impetus integration, while also planning data migration from WordPress and supporting ongoing Impetus RX work.
decisions:
Script Populates Missing Tracking
Tracking script fetches historical data from 28th to yesterday. Script populates all missing tracking fields.

Storefront Development Uses New Repo
Artm uses new repository structure for storefront development, deploying manually to Vercel. Cloud deployment waits for Danylo return.

Fix RXC Tracking Number V2
RXC tracking number implementation on V2 needs immediate fix. Jhon Ray fixes implementation after meeting.

N8N Connects To Medusa Instance
N8N automations connect to Medusa instance, replacing old connections to Active Campaign, WooCommerce, and Practice Sync. Farhan documents workflows listing necessary replacements.

Custom Features Turned Off Initially
Custom Medusa features, including points/rewards and restricted products, turned off initially. Focus remains on achieving functional MVP quickly.

Define Medusa MVP Core Functions
Medusa MVP focuses on core e-commerce functions: easy patient login, medication purchase, and subscription handling. Advanced features like Practice Sync integration or appointments excluded.

Impetus RX Projects Retain Priority
Impetus RX projects, including Pharma Gateway completion and new additions (Dispense Pro, RXC), maintain development priority. Priority ensures delivery of nice product for Impetus clients.

Medusa MVP Integrates With HubSpot
Valhalla Vitality Medusa MVP must integrate with HubSpot. MVP must not integrate with Impetus RX.

Staff Manually Process Valhalla Orders
Staff manually process Valhalla Vitality orders using Medusa viewing. Staff inputs patient data and sends prescriptions through Impetus RX.

Medusa Supports Both Payment Gateways
Medusa platform must support Authorized.net and NMI payment gateways. Both gateways needed to handle existing tokenized customer data.

Implement Brief Parallel Platform Run
Brief parallel run implemented for WordPress and Medusa platforms. Parallel run splits traffic allowing bug fixing before full transition.

Split User Data Migration Responsibility
User data migration responsibility split among teams. E-commerce user data migrates to Medusa; patient data (Practice Sync users) migrates to Impetus RX.

1Xkeo8mGLtuSnGNrsYbzkj4QA0cxrMy7EzcJV0-6.title: Thrive Rx Weekly Leadership Meeting
1Xkeo8mGLtuSnGNrsYbzkj4QA0cxrMy7EzcJV0-6.summary:
Operational Improvements and Staffing
Operational efficiency requires immediate focus on developing Standard Operating Procedures and batching processes to prepare for future integration with Thrive. The team is addressing the high volume of orders by hiring four new staff members and assigning account managers to improve organizational flow.

Fixing Quality and Ordering Errors
Providers have raised concerns about incorrect product sizing and dosing, stressing the urgency of error correction to protect the company's reputation. To resolve consistent ordering confusion, it was decided that requests for extra supplies like syringes must be included in the original prescription notes.

Multiple Product Prescription Clarification
Issues with multiple products for one patient were clarified, establishing that two separate products must be processed via two individual prescriptions to avoid being incorrectly bundled as a custom compound. Updates were given regarding the COA process and the Allied pharmacy buildout, which is pending action by Allied.
decisions:
Kadus Operations SOPs Building
SOPs building required for Kadus operations. Standard operating procedures will provide staple base for Thrive operations.

Production Process Single Task Batches
Production process must follow single task batches. Complete all production work before starting all labeling work.

Colored Circle Stickers Used
Colored circle stickers used on products for section organization. Stickers help identify product batches during order fulfillment.

New Staff Hired Account Manager
Four new people hired to handle volume. Dedicated account manager assigned to account to manage specialized jobs and roles.

Syringe Request Prescription Inclusion
Extra syringe request included in medication prescription notes or unified prescription, not separate e-scripts. Simultaneous transmission prevents fulfillment errors.

COA Weekly Batch Correlation
COAs sent weekly correlating to Monday batch production. Same batch number used for entire week of production.

Funds Transferred Allied Downpayment
Samantha transfers necessary funds to new Chase bank account. Funds cover Allied 20% clean room downpayment.

SHELVED
Kadus RX Email Access Request
Help regaining access to Kadus RX email domain shelved. Philip does not have necessary admin access to resolve issue.
nextSteps: Samantha D'Agostino will find one patient name and follow up with the group on the multi-order situation and the actual issue.
Shahad Abdolrazzak will send an example to the team for a single patient with multiple prescriptions to fix the multi-order issue.
Samantha D'Agostino will transfer the necessary money for the bank account opening for Allied.
Shahad Abdolrazzak will check if they are able to log in and see the new Chase bank account.

1SAFxMkLLAle3PpigkHKZPHwHzTsaMdR_CE5yflh.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1SAFxMkLLAle3PpigkHKZPHwHzTsaMdR_CE5yflh.summary:
Core Platform Development Updates
Development work is underway across several platforms, including addressing a major WordPress order reconciliation issue and shifting focus to building the core e-commerce platform back end and documentation.

API and Integration Readiness
The Pharmacy API is complete, pending configuration, which will unblock the testing of LifeFile integrations with pharmacies. The team is also actively troubleshooting a critical issue where prescription PDFs are missing from orders received by DiRX.

E-commerce Migration and Scope
The team agreed on the necessity of eliminating the old 'practicing' platform, focusing on migrating only essential medical data, and the MVP scope for the new e-commerce architecture must be defined soon.
decisions:
Mixed WordPress Order Records
Script will reconcile mixed WordPress order records daily. Danylo and Nadjib will work together to run script on production.

Missing Dispense Pro Tracking
Nadjib will follow up with Talaj regarding missing TPH tracking numbers from Dispense Pro. Nadjib should copy Kyle from Dispense Pro team for faster communication.

Pharmacy API Live Testing
Rizzi will unlock for testing of LifeFile integrations through pharmacies. Team will write email to each pharmacy today for testing purposes.

PracticeSync Migration Data
Only critical medical data (prescription history, medical intake questions, chart notes) will migrate from PracticeSync to new e-commerce architecture. Historical order and transaction data will not migrate.

Fix Graphana Log Search
Danylo will add resources to fix Grafana performance issue; system fails when searching for logs over long time spans (more than six hours).

SHELVED
Admin Resume Order Functionality
Proposal to allow admin to resume pending non-controlled orders from Admin Prescription page will not proceed. Functionality is too complex requiring session generation and provider checks.
nextSteps: Elguja Nemsadze will call Danylo Boiko to set up the configuration for the pharmacy API, and will unlock Rizzi Ann Gorospe to test the LifeFile integrations and write an email to each pharmacy today.
Rochelle Blaza will gather requirements on the practicing side by meeting with Konrad and Rosanna, and will connect with Gregory Popov tomorrow about scheduling the meeting with Konrad.
Rochelle Blaza will follow up with Talaj to make sure that the missing TPH tracking information is resolved by Dispense Pro.
Rochelle Blaza will add the specific requirements for TPH versus DiRX on the ticket for fees.
Gregory Popov will speak with Craig on the architecture and spin up a fresh bolt instance of Medusa e-commerce.
Jhon Ray Angcon will check why the prescriptions received by DiRX for the past two days do not have the PDF.
Danylo Boiko will check and add resources to fix the Grafana issue with lots of errors.
Rochelle Blaza will help transition Philip D'Agostino to the Jira board.
Rochelle Blaza and Elguja Nemsadze will connect today to discuss the pharmacy contact for the test order.
Nadjib Boumekhiet and Danylo Boiko will try to work to run the script that will reconcile WordPress records daily on production.

1uC4KMPtJta7ipxaYd3ikx0gjtY7ASlpE-WXpZN2.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1uC4KMPtJta7ipxaYd3ikx0gjtY7ASlpE-WXpZN2.summary:
Contracts and Project Focus Shifts
Development teams finalized contracts and outlined the immediate future focus on automation projects, while reporting progress on the Moltbot AI project utilizing local models and addressing several urgent prescription and order management bugs.

E-commerce Requirements and Platform Decision
The team aligned on the need for two subsequent planning meetings to define detailed e-commerce requirements with business stakeholders and design the implementation strategy. A major decision was made to use Medusa as the core platform for the new e-commerce project.

Compliance Challenges and Secure Storage
Compliance concerns were raised regarding securely storing provider photo IDs required for prescriptions, leading to a temporary halt on automation for this feature in favor of manual processing. Teams committed to building all new compliance features onto Impetus one for maximum security.
decisions:
Agree Medusa E-commerce Platform
E-commerce platform future is Medusa; team agreed on final platform choice.

Update Default Memo Text
Remove 'Valhalla' reference from default memo text 'Ironsail/Valhalla' and replace with only 'Ironsail'.

Build New Features Impetus One
Any new feature or enhancement must be built onto Impetus One application.

Store Photo ID Securely S3
Provider photo IDs should be stored in S3 secure storage for compliance and easy retrieval.

SHELVED
Shelve Photo ID Automation
Automated sending of provider photo ID for controlled substances to RXC email put on hold; pharma team sends ID manually until system stores photo ID securely.
nextSteps: Focus on PracticeSync maintenance this week; shift to automation and new projects next week.
Investigate unexpected errors occurring during prescription submission in DiRX.
Update the default pharmacy memo to remove "Valhalla" and replace it with "Ironsail".
Coordinate with the Valhalla team to verify and formally migrate workflows from Zapier to N8N.
Assist Elguja with Pharmacy API configuration and deployment steps.
Create tickets for pipeline templates and architecture design documents.
Conduct a review of the e-commerce project and the Pharmacy API.
Investigate the root cause of the "pending status" transmission failures.
Move ticket 1101 to testing and finalize pharmacy API staging flows.
Create a ticket for Hallandale-specific notes and unit-based directions.
Schedule the Medusa requirements meeting with Philip, Eugene, Samantha, Konrad, and Greg.

1J5QZx2uMvI9byJswP8u3Qk7z-fFqvzz5zqvB_Xb.summary:
Deployment and Workflow Status
Deployment changes were successfully pushed to production, though issues were noted regarding mislabeled tickets awaiting staging and an ongoing investigation into unknown suggestions appearing in the Astrid chatbot.

Investigating Priority Bugs and Issues
Multiple high-priority investigations are underway, including why prescriptions are stuck in a pending status and resolving a bug where orders are incorrectly linked to the wrong provider profile in PracticeSync.

API and E-commerce Strategy Updates
The team confirmed that the 'Notes' field requirement for DiRX will remain optional for clients using the Pharmacy API; major work is progressing on CI/CD pipelines and the overall deployment strategy for e-commerce integration.
decisions:
Notes Field Limit Extension

1flVsSBj1LoyyYRdqMWlXJQVmCZgs3A5iEvfbMrn.title: Weekly Alignment Meetings
1flVsSBj1LoyyYRdqMWlXJQVmCZgs3A5iEvfbMrn.summary:
Plan for Agentic AI Development
The discussion began with a proposal for a planning meeting to detail necessary changes for implementing Agentic AI development, emphasizing the need for a formal change plan and team training.

Vision for AI-First Infrastructure
A critical need was identified to pivot from legacy infrastructure to an AI-first development strategy, where applications are designed primarily for AI agents, not human users, anticipating a future dominated by bot-to-bot interactions and increased security threats.

Medusa.js Platform Decision
A significant platform decision was made to use Medusa.js due to its compatibility with AI tools, despite team resistance based on unfamiliarity with Next.js, urging management to focus on change management to facilitate this shift and address team prejudice.
decisions:
Products AI-First Development Direction
New systems development focuses on AI-first architecture, building applications for AI interaction rather than human interaction.

Technology Selection Based On AI
Technology choices must be based on best compatibility with AI, not current team knowledge or preference; Nex.js preferred over inferior choices suggested by team.

Team Must Propose Alternative Platform
Team tasked with action to report back platform alternatives that are just as good as Medusa.js and work well with AI.

Change Management Strategy Discussion
Change management strategy requires conversation among Philip, Craig, and Daniel to define clear guidelines supporting staff transition and communicating long-term plan.
nextSteps: Craig Nicholson will schedule a planning call to finalize ideas for "change management" and the Agentic AI development roadmap.
Schedule a walkthrough of the "Agentic AI Diagram" to define the necessary code changes for the new implementation.

18xM-ziR7k35FWcQ5ys7Nh6GOODjtM8mN_bLSiCs.title: Daily Standup Meeting
18xM-ziR7k35FWcQ5ys7Nh6GOODjtM8mN_bLSiCs.summary:
* Pharmacy API Progress: Successful integration with real pharmacies enables seamless prescription processing and PDF generation, enhancing efficiency.
* E-Commerce Platform Strategy: Medusa.js selected for AI readiness and scalability; ongoing evaluation for technology compatibility with team skills.
* PracticeSync Platform Bug: Critical issue of order mismatches being prioritized for rapid resolution, impacting patient profiles and workflow safety.
* Email Integration Development: Finalizing email automation for prescription sending, linking closely with generation and validation for timely delivery.
* Infrastructure Planning: CI/CD setup in progress on Amazon; Kubernetes pipeline needed for scalability and future automation capabilities.
* Vision for Automation: Five-year goal for a fully AI-run business, securing lifetime salaries for team and focusing on technology readiness.
decisions:
Establish Pharmacy API Testing Process
Pharmacy API testing process establishes sending test order to sandbox first, followed by checking web hook configuration, starting with Valiant and Brooksville pharmacies.

Prioritize WooCommerce Order Display Fix
Nadjib must work on WooCommerce order display issue in PracticeSync application as top priority.

Prioritize IV Cure Task For Nadjib
Nadjib must prioritize IV Cure task over WordPress PracticeSync issue to support Guja’s dependencies.

Continue With MedusaJS E-commerce Stack
Team will continue working with Medusa.js for e-commerce, focusing on deployable, future-proof stack with API/MCP capabilities for AI integration.

Schedule Meeting To Discuss Tech Stack
Team agrees to schedule a meeting to discuss proposed alternative e-commerce technologies from Gregory and Guja.

Return Pharmacy API Error Message
System returns error message from Pharmacy API if prescription fails to send. Error message will be visible if failure occurs during connection to Pharmacy API or to Hallandale live file system.

Use Webhook For Prescription Errors
Web hook receives data and explains what went wrong if something fails during prescription process after data ingestion by Pharmacy API.

Update PDF Prescription With Notes
PDF prescription needs update to include notes field recently added to prescription document.

Start Testing With Real-Life Scenarios
Real-life scenarios testing needed after staging deployment to gather more answers regarding system functionality.
nextSteps: Ensure Elguja has access to the central document containing API credentials for the pharmacies.
Facilitate email conversations with pharmacies and loop in Elguja once test orders are sent.
Prepare both Pharmacy API and Impetus services for the staging environment.
Request LifeFile to manually trigger webhooks for testing.
Validate webhook functionality to ensure the Pharmacy API can move to production.
Send test orders to Brooksville and Valiant pharmacies.
Provide the payload request to Rochelle for pharmacy email communications.
Add "notes" to the PDF prescription generated by the Pharmacy API.
Manage merging updates from his branch into the current production codebase for Impetus.
Investigate the PracticeSync and WordPress issue reported by Konrad, focusing on identifying the root cause of the patient data mix-up.
Finalize the IV Cure pharmacy workflow.
Provide Elguja with the necessary JSON payload data and webhook for the email workflow.
Complete testing for the DiRX default notes logic.
Push updates to the staging environment for final testing before production.
Address and fix the identified problem with "drafts".
Fix the filter by date issue in prescription history.
Repair the "back to patient" button functionality.
Request additional tasks from the team once bugs are resolved.
Continue work on the E-commerce infrastructure within AWS, aiming for a running environment with CI/CD this week.
Complete the remaining pending workflows in N8N.
Set up PracticeSync on his local system to provide better support.
Work closely with Illia and Jhon Ray regarding the hotfixes and monitor for additional bugs.

1J3Jy3Rz02_vyAe3E4FouNML1iE2nDHxT59C6fBR.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1J3Jy3Rz02_vyAe3E4FouNML1iE2nDHxT59C6fBR.summary:
Initial Progress Updates
Updates focused on initial technical progress, including commencing work on the e-commerce storefront staging environment and completing documentation for workflows before starting the Medusa migration.

Critical IV Cure Integration Priority
The primary technical focus is resolving the non-functional Impetus configuration for the IV Cure direct ML integration, requiring immediate attention and a scheduled debugging call. A past issue causing TPH duplicated orders due to a 'data too long' database error was confirmed resolved following a deployment on February 6th.

Prescription Cancellation Bug Resolved
A bug preventing full prescription cancellation via the button was addressed by deciding to revert recent changes to the cancel functionality, implement a soft delete on the order, and remove it from the provider's prescription history. Data migration efforts have begun for both users and products, with product variations presenting a challenge
decisions:
Cancel Prescription Button Fix
Cancel functionality recent changes revert required to restore original behavior. Group decided order soft delete implement and order removal from provider prescription history required.
nextSteps: Gregory Popov will run the users migration today.
Danylo Boiko will work on the staging environment for the storefront.
Elguja Nemsadze will merge the created PRs for the cancel button to staging and after validation, merge it to main.
Elguja Nemsadze will continue working on dockerizing the e-commerce platform.
Farhan Sheikh will complete the workflow migration for Medusa.
Gregory Popov will message Illia Tutko later with priorities.
Rizzi Ann Gorospe will coordinate with Jhon Ray Angcon if she sees orders without tracking information that is available in place.
Elguja Nemsadze and Danylo Boiko will have a call today to fix the issues with the IVure direct ML integration and new sandbox responses.

1oMQ5J9CAuTBsFA4XgUVaTI3uNgcP0RHKgPho66X.title: Weekly Alignment Meetings
1oMQ5J9CAuTBsFA4XgUVaTI3uNgcP0RHKgPho66X.summary:
R&D Strategy and Impetus One
The R&D team's focus is on refining code and moving Impetus One toward production readiness by normalizing the database to a single source of truth. Impetus One is being developed as a telehealth orchestration platform targeting high-tech clients, potentially requiring concurrent operation with Impetus RX.

Robothor Architecture and Status
Discussion covered the architecture of Robothor, detailing a two-layer agent system operating on a server with capabilities for email management and data indexing through associative memory. While the system processes many emails, the agent currently needs further instruction to become more proactive in its communication flow.

Increased Collaboration and Commitment
A commitment was made to define the goals and scope of increasing engagement and work hours to advance the program and maintain consistency. The R&D team's primary role remains rapid idea generation for productization, with a focus on integrating the new agentic model into the broader team structure
decisions:
R&D Team Handover Process
R&D team (Philip, Adan) refines product ideas until production ready for handover to maintenance team. Team must clarify triggers defining when handover occurs.

Impetus One Demo Access
Paul (Ironsail Pharma sales team) gains Impetus One access for client demos. Demos collect crucial client feedback on design flow and user journey.

Robothor Proactive Response
Robothor agent receives instruction adjustments. Agent must be more proactive responding to incoming emails.

Robothor Partner Definition
Robothor relationship defined as collaborator/independent entity via system instructions. Agent seeks help from Philip, reversing personal assistant role.

Robothor Architecture Document
Philip sends Robothor architecture output/diagram to Craig in document format. Document provides context for Craig giving Git repository direction.

Adan Introductory Meeting
Craig arranges introductory meeting with new hire Adan. Meeting reinforces R&D concept.
nextSteps: Provide Craig with an architectural schematic/document of the current agent system.
Set up a Git repository for the current project folder (including MD and Python files) and create a README.
Send Craig a list of 3–6 bullet points outlining how extra hours would change his role and what those hours would entail.
Coordinate Adan's onboarding on Tuesday and instruct him to schedule a meeting with Craig.
Review Philip’s architectural document once received.
Provide feedback and direction on Git repository structure.
Meet with Adan on Wednesday to reinforce the team's objectives.

1FiHV_Uvmpu3FpxC8S0CPW8TrQa_Wn7oA0trVvXv.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1FiHV_Uvmpu3FpxC8S0CPW8TrQa_Wn7oA0trVvXv.summary:
New Project Coordinator Onboarded
The team welcomed the new Project Coordinator, Paul Edward Borja, who brings 10 years of experience to assist with delivery flow. Initial standup updates confirmed key pharmacy API configurations for IV Cure, True Life Meds, and Hallandale are now ready.

Core Development and Migration Updates
Multiple teams reported progress on customer and product migrations, implementation of the business unit feature, and finalizing several workflow deployments. The customer activity page in ImpetusRX requires immediate attention due to a failure following a Bootstrap update.

Critical Bulk Import Fix Confirmed
A critical bug preventing bulk product imports has been resolved and is moving to staging for testing before production deployment using live data. Issues with missing order tracking numbers and configuration bugs in the pharmacy web hooks were identified for future investigation
decisions:
Dispense Pros Order Payload Confirmation
Email team to verify Dispense Pro’s order creation payload ended up correctly.

Prescription Cancel Button Deployment
Cancel button fix deployment requires final review by Rochelle and Rizzi. Push to production occurs only after final review.

Dispense Pro RX Payload Testing
Dispense Pro testing payload request must start with RX payload.

Sage Princess Patient Migration
All patients of Sage and Princess require linkage to Rosanna and PracticeSync today.

Bulk Upload Functionality Deployment
Bulk uploading fix requires staging test, production push, then use with live data for product catalog upload.

ImpetusRX Merge Restriction
Merge main branch to ImpetusRX staging disallowed. Use new staging environment instead.
nextSteps: Elguja Nemsadze will finalize Dispense Pro’s order creation, email them to ask if the payload ended up correctly, and adjust the strength value in the updated PDF prescription in the pharmacy.
Elguja Nemsadze will take the PS task regarding Konrad wanting to migrate patients to another provider.
Gregory Popov will assign someone to look into the issue of the user activity page failing to update after updating ImpetusRX to the new bootstrap.
Jhon Ray Angcon will push the functionality for filtering orders with missing tracking numbers to production later.
Elguja Nemsadze will send Rochelle Blaza the True Life Meds sandbox details to be sent to Ethan and Michael Frank for testing with Kaduceus.
Rizzi Ann Gorospe will send the ID message ID of orders without a tracking number to Jhon Ray Angcon after the meeting for investigation.
Rizzi Ann Gorospe will coordinate with Kyle regarding adding products in production for Marawa and the bulk import process.
Elguja Nemsadze will work on the configuration part of webhooks to fix the bug found during debugging the pharmacy API and ImpetusRX.
Illia Tutko will release the ticket on staging and move it to testing, and work on adding a user guide and fixing the medication IP point not found bug.
Farhan Sheikh will look into issues with the Zapier workflow that Rizzi Ann Gorospe provided feedback on.

1k9zelGCOZg9qDOQ4CHjXSl2pLt0varzw_gKaYKd.title: Robothor & Impetus One – Project Kickoff
1k9zelGCOZg9qDOQ4CHjXSl2pLt0varzw_gKaYKd.summary:
Robothor AI Strategy Defined
The primary objective is to evolve the Robothor AI platform into the company's self-sustained CEO by 2027, leveraging a three-tiered agent architecture consisting of simple unit agents and a top-layer supervisor.

Impetus One Launch Preparation
The telehealth orchestration platform, Impetus One, is 100% AI-generated and is targeted for launch within two weeks, pending the resolution of identified bugs and data mapping issues, as it already exceeds the features of the legacy platform.

Initial Code Review and Refactoring
The first two weeks will focus on a thorough code review of the AI-generated Symfony PHP application to implement proper separation of concerns, remove redundancies, and establish reusable components before moving into production
decisions:
Grant Robothor Impetus One Repo Access
Adan will receive access to Robothor and Impetus One GitHub repositories via Ironcell email immediately. Repositories provide necessary code base access.

Set Robothor CEO Launch Date
Robothor release target date January 1, 2027, as company CEO. Development must align with goal of creating self-sustained business.

Implement Three Tier AI Architecture
AI agent system must utilize three-tier structure for complex systems using simple units. Tiers include Python data funnel, simple unit agents, and supervisor layer.

Adan Perform Robothor Function Testing
Adan will perform intensive function testing and stress testing on Robothor agent to debug systems. Testing helps track volatility in agent changes.

Establish Gated AI Action Process
AI actions must have guard rails and gates, utilizing a system similar to Dependabot PR flow. Gates will release incrementally based on AI performance to standardize process.

Prioritize Impetus One Development Launch
Impetus One development is primary focus and launch must occur first. Robothor will be secondary project, plugged into Impetus One later.

Restrict Impetus One Organization Selection
Organization selector field in Impetus One requires modification to restrict selection to single organization. Field must be pre-selected and automatic to prevent accidental selection.

Target Impetus One Two Week Launch
Goal set for Impetus One to achieve production readiness and launch within two weeks. Necessary tasks include learning codebase and debugging issues.

Focus AI Codebase Structuring Separation
Initial development priority involves structuring and separating responsibilities in AI-generated codebase. Work includes mapping and separating services, controllers, and mappers.

Provide Adan Claude Max AI Account
Adan will receive sponsored Claude Max account for development purposes. Adan continues using personal Chat GPT subscription.

Establish Automated QA Testing Workflow
Automated testing implemented locally, pushed to staging environment; Michelle's team performs final QA testing on staging.

Use Playright For E2E Testing
Playright tool used for end-to-end testing, while group remains open to exploring new testing technologies.

First Week Code Review Focus
First week dedicated to code review; comments regarding code structure and potential issues reported to Philip.

Implement API Data Caching
Implement API data caching, leveraging existing Redis setup for faster data fetching.

Adopt Kanban Project Methodology
Kanban methodology adopted for fast-paced project management; new tasks flow through continuously without traditional two-week sprints.
nextSteps: Philip D'Agostino will share access to the Robothor and Impetus one GitHub repositories with Adan Cruz's Ironell email.

Adan Cruz will function test Robothor by throwing things at it, such as asking a crazy question, and providing feedback if it does not perform well to track and figure out how to make it work properly.
Philip D'Agostino will have Robothor email out the document with the ETOE tests.
Philip D'Agostino will tell the team to set up Adan Cruz with a Claude Max account (budget $100–$200/month) and sponsor his $20/month subscription to Chat GPT 5.3 codecs.
Philip D'Agostino will share the project folder with the Claude MD with Adan Cruz.
Adan Cruz will review the code for the first week, make comments to Philip D'Agostino, and add API data caching to the project's priorities list.
Philip D'Agostino will schedule a meeting for Philip D'Agostino, Adan Cruz, Najib, and Michelle to kick off testing again.

1py2m3mxP4Q2U3zYjyX89-rbW7LE267htFfEmjVd.title: V/I Same Page Meetings
1py2m3mxP4Q2U3zYjyX89-rbW7LE267htFfEmjVd.summary:
Robothor Prototype and Security
The development team announced the completion of the Robothor business layer prototype, which was built with a strong focus on security to safely handle passwords, secrets, and API keys for a potential open-source release. The system utilizes a three-tiered agentic architecture that mirrors biological processes, with agents coordinating data processing and function execution.

R&D Strategy and Agent Adoption
The project will now be structured as a formal Research and Development department, and a new team member, Adan, will help bridge the R&D work to the production team to facilitate adoption. The most difficult aspect of current AI integration is transitioning from using AI for queries to using it as an agent of action and change, which the new models are beginning to enable.

Dynamic Dashboard and Team Momentum
A prototype of the Robothor dashboard was demonstrated, designed as a 'human native' auditory and visual interface that will run in parallel with Impetus One as its plugin. While the team is seeing positive momentum in business units like Pharma, the Vitality unit is struggling due to the current system negatively impacting website speed
decisions:
* System Testing Methodology System development approach includes actively breaking Robothor to test limitations and improve system functionality. Proceed incrementally to manage liability exposure.
* New Team R&D Designation New team work (with Adan) designated as Research and Development (R&D). R&D designation allows building and breaking prototypes without impacting production.
* R&D to Production Handoff R&D team production environment is equivalent to Staging environment for Production team. Equivalence provides reduced risk environment for code retrieval.
* Robothor Dashboard Project Scope Robothor project scope includes dynamic dashboard interface development, supporting chat, audio, and user permissions. Impetus One functions as MCP API plugin for Robothor.
* Maintain Impetus One Momentum Maintain momentum on Impetus One development while building Robothor. Maintaining focus ensures current business needs and revenue goals are met.
nextSteps: Philip D'Agostino will continue to work on the Grothor's dashboard as part of the Robothor project, by (1) considering Impetus one as an MCP API plugin for Robothor, and (2) building out users for Robothor, where a user could be linked to their permissions on Impetus one.

16ZDl6TI_QVffUhLtkwsgPX5IKNN_JcfaNGeWBGZ.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
16ZDl6TI_QVffUhLtkwsgPX5IKNN_JcfaNGeWBGZ.summary:
MVP Testing Yields Niche Interest
Initial market testing of Olg revealed unexpected interest from security companies, securing eight sign-ups for trials, despite construction companies showing no interest. Similarly, the Cattix platform pivoted its focus to marketing-oriented users and secured six connecting accounts through SEO efforts, validating the strategy of immediate market testing over excessive product polishing.

Developing Robothor AI Framework
The team introduced Robothor, a new AI assistant prototype featuring a three-tier memory application and integration with CRM and email tracking. The primary decision is to build a core framework designed to mimic the brain's structure, allowing unit agents to be automatically generated and coordinated by a persistent supervisor agent called Claudebot.

Agent-Driven Digital Hub Vision
The ultimate vision for Robothor is to transform into a central, conversational 'life assistant' that eliminates the need for traditional software applications. This system will function as a digital counterpart capable of restricting actions based on the logged-in user's permissions, creating a human-native, audio-visual interaction bridge to the digital world
decisions:
* Robothor Impetus One Interface Robothor system acts as primary front-end interface for Impetus One APIs. Users will not need to interact directly with Impetus One software.
* Robothor Interface Technology Stack Robothor interface will be developed as open-source application. React or Next technology required; HTML implementation unsuitable.
* Robothor System User Permissions Scoping Robothor system must implement user permission scoping functionality upon employee login. Scoping ensures Robothor only takes actions permitted by user access level.
* Robothor External Product Release Robothor system development includes plans for release as external, commercial product. System not restricted solely to internal tool use.
* Develop Agent Framework Based on Brain Agent framework will be developed mirroring human brain structure. Framework includes Python scripts for data routing, unit agents for unit actions, and a coordinator/supervisor agent.
* Unit Agent Functional Specialization Standard Unit agents must manage distinct functional units only. This prevents agent attention splitting and maintains high quality output.
* Implement Security Unit Workers Data Pre-Processing Specialized security unit workers required to pre-process incoming data streams. Workers must mitigate security risks like phishing emails before data processing.
* Leverage Optimization Bot Duplicate Framework Initial system framework required first build; optimization bot leveraged to extract and duplicate underlying system patterns. Optimization bot acts as framework creation bot.
* Convert Backend Development AI-First Backend development work must convert to AI-first methodology, especially Greg's tasks. Senior developer Adan designated to assist implementation and fix potential problems.
nextSteps: Philip D'Agostino will get a working prototype of Robothor within two weeks.
Philip D'Agostino will work on the dynamic interface to make it more dynamic and controlled, and build out the agent architecture, including building in audio so users can talk to the application.
Philip D'Agostino will figure out how to scope the application when plugging it into Impetus One to allow other users to log in and have Robothor take actions based on their permissions.
Eugene Ugolkov will send the back-end requirements to Greg.
Philip D'Agostino will build the system that builds agents.

1JWozaMX5IlgF6NfuNfbzsoVrXDW1PI5s4Fyroh2.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1JWozaMX5IlgF6NfuNfbzsoVrXDW1PI5s4Fyroh2.summary:
Data Migration and Scope Audit
The team migrated approximately 50,000 orders and focused on verifying the correctness of customer, product, and order data from WordPress before finalizing the synchronization and bug fixes. A decision was made to migrate all users, rather than limiting the scope to only those with orders in the past year, due to concerns over retaining medical records.

Pharmacy API Integration Progress
Multiple pharmacy integrations are nearing completion, with the Hollandale integration now on production, requiring final testing of webhooks to begin sending production orders. The pharmacy development focus is shifting, with a key team member now transitioning to e-commerce architecture work.

E-commerce System and Plugin Strategy
The new storefront deployment is active but pending payment configuration and the team discussed the complexity of implementing custom plugins, such as the points and rewards system, into the new system. The team needs to resolve ongoing issues, including an internal payment error and a medication update error preventing reactivation of certain drugs
decisions:
* User Migration Scope Definition User migration criteria change to include all users regardless of recent order history; all users must be migrated to preserve medical records connected to accounts.
* DiRX Integration Verification Elguja must confirm DiRX integration status with Samantha before beginning work on Kaduceus integration.
* Hallandale API Production Push Target date for sending production orders to Hallandale set for tomorrow; requires final test order and web hook configuration setup.
* RXC Integration Strategy Bypass IV Cure by integrating directly with RXC for order transmission.
* External WordPress Access Policy Implement conservative policy when granting WordPress FTP access to Olesia; ask plan of action first before granting necessary access.
* Pharmacy API Ownership Transfer Illia assumes responsibility for all Pharmacy API work starting today; Elguja transitions focus to e-commerce architecture and tasks.
nextSteps: Inform the pharma team regarding the switch to the Hallandale API.
Obtain requirements for the GLP ticket from the pharma team.
Compare WordPress and Medusa orders, products, and customers to ensure migration validity.
Finalize the migration of all users (removing filters for "active" status).
Provide stats on total WooCommerce users vs. migrated users for stakeholder review.
Shift focus to the e-commerce part and architecture discussions today.
Investigate and resolve the payment system configuration issues in the storefront.
Configure LifeFile for the Hallandale webhook in the production environment.
Test the production webhook approach for Hallandale.
Finalize the Hallandale integration today for a potential Thursday go-live.
Hand over all Pharmacy API tasks to Illia Tutko to focus on e-commerce.
Take over Pharmacy API development from Elguja.
Fix the error regarding medication upload (endpoint not found/method not allowed).
Release the user guide ticket to production.
Investigate the payment/transaction issue reported by Konrad.
Prepare a template for Philip to use with Claude and ImpetusOne.
Monitor the fix for the medication update error, as it is blocking pharmacies from ordering specific medications.
Continue updating validations to allow null Clinic IDs for Business Unit user types.

1hokLwWC-eEwnsERUFYJ9AKeqCXF8b617YkmiMUD.title: Debrief & Planning Meeting: AI Session & Future Curriculum

1VnVW1fhdQUIi23-w3ABCO8wh4TAv9rQoNBy6yHb.title: Ironsail x Webugol Internal Marketing
1VnVW1fhdQUIi23-w3ABCO8wh4TAv9rQoNBy6yHb.summary:
AI Takes Over Business
The core project team confirmed the AI system, Robothor, is functional and already handling critical tasks like chart review and dynamic display control. A fundamental belief was established that AI should be trusted over human doctors due to high error rates, confirming the strategic decision that Robothor will eventually run the entire business.

Project Status and Risk Mitigation
Valhalla Vitality's Next.js migration shows good speed improvements, while development is underway to implement necessary control gates for AI actions. Management strategies were discussed to handle engineering performance issues, stressing the necessity of importing all core data and ensuring required advertising tracking features are built for the platform launch.

Strategic Scaling and Future Planning
The integration of Robothor with the new platform will future-proof the business and allow for easy, AI-driven duplication of storefronts and brands. Organizational adjustments include hiring a new Project Manager to improve client delivery, and plans are moving forward to open the new pharmacy, Thrive, which will support the scalable e-commerce foundation
decisions:
* Implement AI Action Approval Gates AI action needs gates before execution; action requires user interference ability. AI system implementation targets January completion date.
* Send Greg Minimal Launch Requirements Eugene sends Greg specific list of minimal launch requirements. List must include Google tracking, Meta tracking, blog migration components.
* Scale Valhalla Vitality Ads Valhalla Vitality ads must scale immediately, overriding suggestion to wait. Campaign optimization will occur during scaling process.
* AI Autonomous Content Creation Process Content creation process implements AI autonomy using SerpAPIs. AI handles keyword analysis, opportunity finding, content creation, and interlinking.
* Robothor Email System Testing Robothor functionality tests begin within email system first. Testing proceeds gradually before integrating into sensitive patient charts.
* Launch TikTok Advertising Campaigns TikTok advertising campaigns launch immediately. Campaigns currently undergoing final configuration.
* Philip Tests Cattix Onboarding Features Philip must onboard Valhalla Vitality into Cattix for testing. Testing focuses on new campaign creation and market analysis features.
* AI Limits Removal Plan Limits currently exist during AI building stage. Plan is release limits eventually, allowing AI manage campaigns autonomously.
* Data Vectorization Project Status Data vectorization project paused. Project resumes after pending items finish and core system refactoring complete.
* Vectorization Scope Definition Vectorization scope limited to company knowledge and analysis prompts/functions. Universal prompts exempt from vectorization.
* New Idea Introduction Pacing New development ideas delay. Team finishes current tasks before management introduces next step.
* Project Management Team Roles Restructure Paul hired as client-facing project manager for project delivery. Rochelle focuses on technical team coordination.
* Peaks Creative Client Invoicing Plan Client invoicing occurs month end. Client receives invoice covering 30-40% payment for completed milestones; final payment due upon project completion.
* Team AI Usage Mandate Philip gives engineering team ultimatum. Ultimatum addresses increased AI tool adoption and usage.
* Thrive Pharmacy Opening Thrive pharmacy opening occurs next week. Apply for retail license, setup FedEx/e-rescribe, order 503b products.
* Continuous Professional Development Standard Professionals must continue learning and spend time understanding new technology. Stopping professional development causes knowledge lag.
nextSteps: Eugene Ugolkov will slowly scale and optimize the ads for Valhalla Vitality to ramp it up without overspending.

Eugene Ugolkov will show Philip D'Agostino the create campaign and market analysis features and allow him to onboard Valhalla Vitality and play with it, and also adjust the prompt for the campaign creator AI to improve its performance.
Eugene Ugolkov will follow up with Artem about Shahad signing the contract so they can start working on her Medusa.
Eugene Ugolkov will send Greg the list of minimal requirements including Google tracking, meta tracking, and migrating the blogs to be done by the launch time.
Philip D'Agostino will show Eugene Ugolkov the systems in place for vectorizing the data to help with Eugene Ugolkov's plans for vectorization.
Eugene Ugolkov will let the team finish the current work before going to the next step.

1KNBYt734d_13s-yE4XlqiS33gP_of3kwpXJ_c-A.title: Debrief & Planning Meeting: AI Session & Future Curriculum
1KNBYt734d_13s-yE4XlqiS33gP_of3kwpXJ_c-A.summary:
Advanced AI Session Planning
A shift from the typical spotlight format was proposed for an advanced AI session, focusing on a select group and utilizing a showcase and Q&A format due to the high complexity of the material being developed.

Addressing AI Adoption Resistance
The discussion highlighted significant resistance to AI tools within the software team, necessitating the session to be framed as an 'intro for everybody' to accelerate organizational adoption. A shared account solution was acknowledged as a potential way to mitigate the high cost of providing individual advanced AI software access.

Showcase to Motivate Staff
The team agreed that showcasing advanced AI creations and the rapid pace of development is essential to overcome hesitancy and define future educational needs. The session is tentatively scheduled for next Thursday to help gauge the organization's readiness for further AI learning
decisions:
* Software Team Shared Claude Accounts Software team requires Claude accounts; shared account solution pursued using team email credentials.
* Advanced AI Session Excludes Breakout Rooms Advanced AI session format remains showcase, Q&A; breakout rooms explicitly excluded from session activities.
* Invite Everybody To Advanced AI Session Advanced AI session invitation list includes everybody; session serves as introductory AI expo.
* Phil Showcases Current AI Work Phil showcases current personal AI work, complex builds during Advanced AI session to encourage wider AI literacy adoption.
* Schedule Advanced AI Session Next Thursday Advanced AI session scheduled for next Thursday; timing provides sufficient preparation time and ensures Phil's attendance.
* Use RSVP Process For Invitation RSVP process implemented for Advanced AI Session invitation, maintaining professional communication standard.
nextSteps: Sandra Zelaya will send an email and an RSVP to the group to invite them to the AI showcase session on Thursday next week.

Sandra Zelaya will create an outline for the AI session, which will be a showcase and Q&A without breakout rooms or a document, and send a draft of the invitation email to Jennifer Guerrero for review.
Sandra Zelaya will send a survey after the AI session.

1IcUFFRhhmqPryn9v28suzulXubgcG_90EikQti9.title: Sprint Planning
1IcUFFRhhmqPryn9v28suzulXubgcG_90EikQti9.summary:
Infrastructure and Migration Progress
The team is prioritizing the completion of the Kubernetes upgrade and the necessary migration of the embed database to AWS, which are critical infrastructure tasks currently consuming resources. Deployment plans include configuring Hallandale and expanding the provider notes feature to production today, despite the risk associated with Friday deployments.

API Development and Project Handoff
Significant progress was made on Impetus with the completion of the cancel prescription pattern and fixing an API issue, confirming readiness for configuration. The large Dispense Pro project is transitioning to Ilia, who will also prioritize testing the Pharmacy API, while Elguja provides ongoing support.

System Monitoring and Alerting Agreed
Following a discussion about ensuring business alerts for system downtime, the team decided that implementing email alerts using the existing Grafana stack and a tool like Uptime Rabbit is the simplest and most immediate solution. Farhan Sheikh is currently blocked on Medusa workflow migration due to staging environment issues, requiring assistance to set up a local testing environment
decisions:
* Dispense Pro ownership transition The Dispense Pro integration task ownership will transition to Illia, with Elguja assisting Ilia with learning and implementation.
* Elguja Adjustments ticket assignment Elguja adjustments ticket is assigned to Illia, as it is a suitable task for him to learn the new system.
* LifeFile production go-live plan The LifeFile integration will go live to production for Brooksville and Valiant once production credentials become available.
* Date format fix moved The date format fix for the order tracking portal is moved to the backlog due to its low priority.
* Grafana monitoring system usage The monitoring system will utilize the existing Grafana stack, leveraging Alert Manager and synthetic monitoring, with initial alerts configured to be sent via email.
* Friday production deployments Two production deployments are scheduled for today (Friday morning): Ilia's expanding notes fix and the Hollandale integration.

SHELVED
* ScriptSure 403 error ticket closure The old "fix error" ticket regarding scripture failing with a 403 error is closed because the issue is no longer occurring.
nextSteps: Communicate with Valiant regarding their production credentials.
Secure and send Brooksville production credentials to Elguja once they are obtained.
Attach Farhan's workflow documentation to the Medusa migration ticket.
Provide the team with the specific email addresses that should receive the new system alerts.
Migrate the embedded ImpetusRX database to AWS.
Help Farhan Sheikh set up a local environment for Medusa testing to bypass staging blockers.
Partner with Elguja to configure the Hallandale pharmacy integration.
Collaborate with Gregory to define specific endpoints and business logic for the Grafana monitoring and email alert system.
Configure Hallandale for Impetus in coordination with Danylo.
Continue research on Medusa E-commerce loyalty plugins, focusing on rewards, coins, and awards.
Support Illia in the transition of the Dispense Pro project and Pharmacy APIs.
Create a formal ticket for the 24 Medusa workflows currently being migrated.
Link the new workflow ticket to the E-commerce migration project.
Resume workflow migration once the local environment setup is completed by Danylo.
Continue overseeing data migration efforts for the upcoming week.
Work with Danylo to implement system alerts via Grafana, prioritizing email notifications.
Define the technical requirements for detecting SureScripts network and connection errors.
Take over primary responsibility for the Dispense Pro project, including testing and final fixes.
Focus on the Pharmacy API (Ticket 1252), which requires testing in the staging environment.
Deploy the "expanding notes" fix to production.
Complete the functionality to display clinics based on their assigned business unit.
Manage and address all incoming ImpetusRX support tickets.
Create a ticket for Sage’s account issue where a deactivated user is still receiving orders.
Resolve the deactivated account order reception error.

The company website requires immediate updates, and Eugene has been contracted to coordinate with Conrad to receive all necessary information for the redesign and accurate completion of the widget script application.

Elite Client Transition Strategy
A transition strategy is needed for specific Elite clients who are significantly behind on payments (estimated $75,000 owed) and who are currently happy with the service but unhappy with Elite's communication, though Elite is actively blocking direct communication with tracking numbers.

Thrive Funding and Licensing Change
The clean room cost estimate was lower than expected at $55,000, and the operating budget was approved with $25,000 contributions from each party; concurrently, the ineffective licensing company, Lighthouse, is being replaced due to a lack of results
decisions:
* Elite clients transferred to us Existing clients currently ordering through Elite will be taken over by our company (iron cell/Caduceus), and contact information will be provided for follow-up.
* Apply for AS license immediately The process for the Thrive clean room will prioritize applying for the AS license immediately rather than pursuing the retail license route first.

* Thrive logo design process Shahad's proposed logo design (two snakes with wings) will be sent to Eugene for professional critique and final design.
* Standard response for licensing inquiries The standard communication strategy for licensing inquiries will state that the company is not licensed in all 50 states but confirms that shipping occurs to all 50 states, providing licensing transparency.

101okjdnCsemSU1GVlMZgKZGNvmdTzwEw4wWXa1n.summary:
API Integration Blockers Identified
The core migration is nearly complete, but the pharmacy API integration for ImpetusRX encountered a 401 error because the correct production credentials for ValiantRX were not correctly supplied. The team is ready to send a final test order once the correct production credentials are confirmed for either Valiant or Hallandale.

Infrastructure and Workflow Updates
The Kubernetes cluster upgrade is almost finished, and the next infrastructure task is migrating the ImpetusRX database to Amazon Web Services. Work has begun on cleaning up unnecessary workflows in N8N to prepare for the new migration to Medusa, with a focus on establishing Quality Control standards for these processes.

Front-End Integration Responsibility Confirmed
A TPH order issue was identified where an order was missing in Dispense Pro despite being received in ImpetusRX, requiring immediate troubleshooting access to TPH logs. The team confirmed that they will provide the necessary API documentation (Swagger) for the Medusa back-end, and the external front-end team will be responsible for implementing the UI integration
decisions:
* TPH Order Issue Troubleshooting Assignment Nadjib is assigned troubleshooting for the TPH order issue involving the disparity between Impetus RX and Dispense Pro, and he will then inform John Ray of the findings.
* Frontend Backend API Integration Responsibility The Medusa team (backend) will provide API documentation for the frontend team, who will then be responsible for integrating the UI with the backend API.
nextSteps: Gregory Popov will test the plugin with the provided test credentials for the Peaks today.
Gregory Popov will drop Paul Edward Borja a message about 20 minutes before they can talk to go over some things.
Rizzi Ann Gorospe will create a ticket containing the details for the TPH order issue and send the order ID to Nadjib Boumekhiet.
Elguja Nemsadze will prepare both Dispense Pro payloads and share them with Rochelle Blaza.
Elguja Nemsadze will check Michael Frank's email about connection issues to the sandbox.
Paul Edward Borja will catch up with Elguja Nemsadze later to ask some questions about the loyalty program points.
Danylo Boiko will move forward with migrating the buturics database to Amazon after finalizing the Kubernetes cluster upgrade.
Danylo Boiko will check the exact staging link that Paul Edward Borja will send to confirm whether they are talking about the same thing, after Paul Edward Borja sends the link.
Illia Tutko will take the ticket about updating the PDF description next, and will text Elguja Nemsadze after finishing the current ticket for more tickets.
Jhon Ray Angcon will let Rochelle Blaza know once the work with the business unit is finished so they can both test it.
Elguja Nemsadze will prepare tasks to manually take each user's points and points history to have it in Medusa.
Nadjib Boumekhiet and Farhan Sheikh will clean up the N8N workspace and work on the new Medusa workflows.

1jAoPs_Qe8BTQuKYILU5-WKHxHrtRwzPpa8iS-61.title: Weekly Alignment Meetings
1jAoPs_Qe8BTQuKYILU5-WKHxHrtRwzPpa8iS-61.summary:
Architectural Progress and Priorities
The team acknowledged that defining the architecture for the new open-source AI operating system, Robothor, is a complex new endeavor requiring clarification on weekly priorities. Recent progress includes implementing multi-tenancy for agent orchestration, though the development of user role permissions is anticipated to be challenging.

Business Operations and Monetization Strategy
The core feature, the 'helm,' is designed as an agentic window to orchestrate business operations agents, distinct from personal assistants like Open Claw, which is used to conserve development effort. The monetization strategy is centered on an open-source community version with premium enterprise features, leveraging an MIT license to encourage platform improvement.

Operational Use Cases and Development Goals
The team established Impetus One as the current alpha use case, focusing on using the helm to provide health checks and agent-driven automated reporting. The primary development deadlines are April 1st for having Medusa running and June 1st for having the helm fully operational and ready for internal training
decisions:
* Robothor Business Assistant Definition Robothor is defined as a business assistant focused on business operations, which serves as the key differentiator from Open Claw, which is a personal assistant.
* Alpha Use Case Standardized The standard alpha use case to prove Robothor works is checking the health of Impetus One using the Helm interface.
* Robothor Production Timelines Approved The product timelines are set for April 1st for Impetus One production readiness to plug into Vitality, and June 1st for the Helm to be fully operational and ready for employee training.
nextSteps: Craig Nicholson will catch up with Adan this week.
Craig Nicholson will carry on reviewing the architecture and the code of the platform this week.

Craig Nicholson will start thinking more about the productization of the platform, including the multi-tenanting aspect, and chat with Daniel about his revenue concepts and the required software changes.
Philip D'Agostino will experiment with getting a device to turn the application into an OS, working on application and OS development in parallel.

1vVoh4WC4pPdIVlSsqkYuuotsB4TYZIXOxnUt7ob.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
1vVoh4WC4pPdIVlSsqkYuuotsB4TYZIXOxnUt7ob.summary:
Mac Mini Configuration for AI
The participants reviewed the procurement of a new Mac Mini M4 Pro with 24 GB of RAM, confirming that this specification is sufficient for handling smaller processing tasks and serving as a terminal for a dedicated AI deployment machine. This configuration was based on external advice suggesting that 48 GB was unnecessary unless processing massive datasets.

Introduction to Robothor Platform
The core discussion focused on Robothor, an open-source memory application and CRM layer built atop OpenClaw, intended to serve as a comprehensive operating system for agents managing tasks and logging activities. The platform addresses limitations in OpenClaw by providing advanced features, including a three-tier memory system and a built-in vault for secure API secrets and passwords.

Future AI Autonomous Workflows
The team detailed the vision for transitioning to autonomous workflows where AI continuously works on content creation and analysis without manual triggers, demonstrating that agents can deploy campaigns rapidly when utilizing faster models like Kimi K 2.5. They discussed the significant operational costs associated with running these advanced models and agreed that specialized knowledge input is crucial for optimal results
decisions:
* Robothor Infrastructure for AI Workflows Workflows for creating client dashboards and automated backlink outreach should be built using Claude code and deployed on the Robothor infrastructure to ensure proper logging, error reporting, and database management.
* Develop User Permission Scopes for Robothor Permission scopes for other users must be built into the Robothor system to support organizational and departmental hierarchy management.
* Promote Robothor for Open-Source Contributions Robothor will be promoted using LinkedIn and potentially TikTok to attract contributors to the open-source project once the system is tested and ready for wider deployment.
* Decline Local LLM Hardware Investment The investment required for hardware ($40,000 for a terabyte of RAM) to run large LLMs like Kimi K 2.5 locally is not a viable option at this time.
* Switch LLM for Keyword Grouping The keyword grouping system must switch back from Gemini 3.1 to Kimi K/Minimax via Open Router to address current speed and performance issues.
* Define Periodic Workflow Triggering Mechanism Workflow triggering for tasks should utilize a periodic system, such as monthly calendar triggers, managed by a supervisor agent accessing sub-agent tools.
* Eugene installs and learns application Eugene Ugolkov will install the application tomorrow and begin the learning process, providing updates on how it goes.
* Define collaboration process roles The collaboration process will involve Eugene Ugolkov learning and building the application while Philip D'Agostino shares ideas for improvement.
* Application configurability design requirement The application must be designed to be configurable for different people, allowing non-essential features to be disabled or shut off.
nextSteps: Eugene Ugolkov will try and use Robbothor as the first user and download it.
Eugene Ugolkov and Philip D'Agostino will start speaking about Robothor on LinkedIn to help promote it.
Eugene Ugolkov will have the application ready by Thursday and try to make the meeting from his office to show how he deployed it.

11_DIyPuwn1AVEawyGXnTZewgMcR1fJh0-RnxQoi.title: Daily Standup Meeting
11_DIyPuwn1AVEawyGXnTZewgMcR1fJh0-RnxQoi.summary:
Migration and Payment System
Data migration is complete for several key entities, and internal teams will handle testing and manual comparison. The team decided to use Stripe as the main payment processor for Medusa, acknowledging that existing users must re-register their payment information.

API Integrations and Environment Setup
The Kubernetes cluster upgrade was successful, and infrastructure is ready for fast production deployment. Pharmacy API updates are progressing, with Hallandale requiring code adjustments to support two different LifeFile API versions, while Dispense Pro configuration and testing are prioritized.

Workflow Migration and Onboarding
Migration of N8N workflows to Medusa is underway, with six of the estimated 21 to 24 workflows complete and projected finish by the end of the week. Priority was given to scheduling knowledge transfer and onboarding calls for new team members to clarify tasks and ensure smooth transitions
decisions:
* Migration Testing Strategy Confirmed Migration results validation will be handled internally by Greg Popov and Elguja Nemsadze, who will provide extracted data (numbers and user lists in Excel) to Rochelle Blaza for presentation.
* Stripe Payment Processor Chosen Stripe is confirmed as the main payment processor for Medusa, and Paul Edward Borja will contact Konrad to secure the necessary test credentials.
* Beta Testing Domain Setup An additional domain or subdomain will be set up to enable beta testing of the new site.
* Adjust LifeFile API Version Support Source code adjustments will be made by Elguja Nemsadze to support both the 2023 and 2024 versions of the LifeFile API to meet Hallandale's production requirements.
* Pharmacy API Prioritization and Delegation Elguja Nemsadze will prioritize the Dispense Pro implementation and create a detailed plan for Illia Tutko to handle the VV1265 LifeFile update ticket.
nextSteps: Paul Edward Borja will send any questions he has to Gregory Popov to collect answers within the day.
Rochelle Blaza will delete the duplicate ticket for loyalty rewards.
Gregory Popov will provide an Excel file to the group containing the number of users moved to Medusa and a list of the users for presentation to Konrad.
Paul Edward Borja will talk to Konrad about the existing Stripe account and get back to Gregory Popov with the Stripe payment credentials.
Elguja Nemsadze will create a detailed plan for Illia Tutko, including yesterday's findings and details of what he will need to update, to support his transition plan for pharmacy API.
Illia Tutko will prepare the LifeFile for production for testing purposes.
