1jddzhlkyX9G_sP63TlSajmt8jNPue8CMGRvBHnM.title: Weekly Senior Leadership Standup
1jddzhlkyX9G_sP63TlSajmt8jNPue8CMGRvBHnM.summary:
Addressing Compliance and Leadership
Following a controlled substance incident, the team emphasized the critical and urgent need for an experienced strategic medical director to establish robust protocols, mitigate liability, and ensure compliance. They discussed compensating the ideal candidate, who could significantly help triple revenue, potentially through a profit-sharing model rather than an equity stake.

Immediate Risk Mitigation Strategies
Short-term operational changes include instructing teams to step away from promoting plain phentermine and focus campaigns on lower-risk mixed phentermine options until a strategic director is hired. Additionally, the team needs to confirm the details of the state tracking system used in their EHR to improve controlled substance tracking immediately.

Finance and System Updates
The tax compilation is underway, and the February close will begin after finalizing the year-end packet, following a successful test transaction with the new Bill system. The migration off the old practicing system was accelerated, now targeted for completion by March 15th to allow for two weeks of critical testing
decisions:
* Use via RX for provider coverage Provider coverage for states lacking a license will be acquired via RX for a $15 flat fee per order, as it is the most cost-effective option.
* CMO Profit Sharing compensation model The new Chief Medical Officer should be incentivized through a profit-sharing model tied to revenue growth rather than being offered an ownership stake of 25%.
* Frame recruitment as Olesia’s idea Recruitment for a strategic medical consultant leader should be framed as Olesia’s idea to focus Rosanna on enhancing protocols for revenue generation and compliance regarding high-converting medications like Phentermine.
* Promote Phentermine mix options The company should add more Phentermine mix options and run marketing campaigns focused on them to promote safer use and compliance, rather than marketing only plain Phentermine.
* Set EHR migration timeline The target date for completing the migration off the current EHR system (PracticeSync) is March 15th, allowing two weeks for testing before the scheduled April 1st rollout.
nextSteps: Konrad Arciszewski will figure out the compensation model for the chief medical director before the meeting with the doctor on Friday.
Caroline Barad will update the Coburn invoices with the updated numbers through January 31st.
Konrad Arciszewski will talk to Olesia and suggest recruiting a medical consultant for protocols and compliance as her idea.
Samantha D'Agostino will send the blurb on Valhalla organization to Caroline Barad so she can send it to the M&A advisors.
Samantha D'Agostino will tell Mazen to reach out to Daniel McCarthy.
Konrad Arciszewski will reach out to Maddie to get the payment processor rate recap and send it to Caroline Barad for her to examine and confirm.

1AUUa06WcNZjiYtR3UsB8_Yr27IuiRsUy9_NG0ZO.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1AUUa06WcNZjiYtR3UsB8_Yr27IuiRsUy9_NG0ZO.summary:
E-commerce Setup and Alerts
The local e-commerce environment was finalized, and testing for email alerts commenced, although one alert ticket was proposed to be put on hold due to an unclear scope. Concurrent work progressed on Dispense Pro, including pharmacy API integration, and on installing necessary skills within Medusa.

Payment Gateway Conflict Erupts
A standard was reinforced requiring complete and detailed ticket descriptions to improve clarity and future troubleshooting capabilities for the team. A significant conflict emerged regarding the e-commerce payment infrastructure, as developers noted the impracticality of merging multiple payment gateways like authorized.net and Stripe due to incompatible token reuse capabilities.

Integration Updates and Workload
Integration tasks are nearing completion, including updates to the PDF prescription file and ongoing work on the Hallandale integration update. The meeting concluded with an acknowledgment of one developer's high cross-domain workload across multiple significant projects, suggesting a need to manage context switching
decisions:
* Detailed Ticket Descriptions Mandate Developers must consistently add detailed and verbose descriptions to all tickets for better understanding, especially for future troubleshooting or onboarding purposes.
* HubSpot SMS Approval Coordination Farhan Sheikh will reach out to Allesia to confirm the activation status of the HubSpot SMS feature after completing the active Medusa workflows.
* Authorized.Net Token Migration Existing tokenized payment data from Authorize.net cannot be reused or migrated to Stripe due to security and EC violation concerns.

SHELVED
* Data Population Ticket On Hold The pending data population ticket will be put on hold until all necessary inputs, which require clarification from Rochelle, are available.
nextSteps: Gregory Popov will speak with Elguja Nemsadze today to give a better overview of the customer migration PR.
Paul Edward Borja will check with Rochelle Blaza on the confusing ticket that is not in progress.
Farhan Sheikh will push the active Medusa workflows to git so Nadjib Boumekhiet can review them.
Farhan Sheikh will reach out to Olesia regarding the SMS activation process on HubSpot after completing the workflows.
Gregory Popov will catch up with Craig, Philip, or Konrad to check on the payment system.
Danylo Boiko will set up the email alerts today and test if it works, and Gregory Popov will provide the list of emails to Danylo Boiko once the alerts are confirmed to be working.
Paul Edward Borja and Elguja Nemsadze will have a separate call later today to discuss Elguja Nemsadze's workload and tasks.

Positive Investor Interest Received
The recent investor meeting was successful, generating confidence for future delivery and potential public listing via a shell company and subsequent audit. Investors showed significant interest in the current peptide focus, acknowledging the long-term potential in personalized medicine supported by data and future AI integration.

Genius OS Adoption Strategy Shift
Discussion shifted to converting the Genius OS concept into revenue by achieving critical open-source adoption, as the team cannot succeed alone. Developing the OS into a full system will require a more powerful AI than currently available and will necessitate starting with a strong business application layer before moving to deeper hardware integration.

Scaling Business and Automation Impact
The strategy centers on using AI for employee leverage to enable infinite business growth at virtually no cost, aiming for market dominance. Compliance and core business operations, such as opening the pharmacy and delivering drugs, must remain on track alongside strategic innovation
decisions:
* Genius OS open source adoption Open source adoption for Genius OS development must be pursued to facilitate multiple angles of probing and development.
* Genius OS application layer start Genius OS development will commence at the application layer before moving into a deeper OS/shell integration.
* Business 80/20 execution balance An 80/20 balance must be maintained, dedicating 80% of effort to delivering current business needs and 20% to experimentation and innovation.
* UK compliance vendor connection A connection with the UK compliance tracking software vendor will be made by Daniel for pharmacy supply chain compliance data management.
nextSteps: Daniel McCarthy will connect Philip D'Agostino with the guy running the compliance tracking software business if he follows up with thoughts.

1h6sr4tfCqG30ChVhXUVorjAaEVs-BgvtuIHiu--.title: Ironsail x Webugol Internal Marketing
1h6sr4tfCqG30ChVhXUVorjAaEVs-BgvtuIHiu--.summary:
Open Claw Configuration and Deployment
The discussion began with challenges encountered during the initial configuration of the open claw, including fixing a crash and successfully deploying the application using Python and self-hosting it locally with 'rock'.

Secure Hosting and Model Strategy
A critical decision was made to enhance deployment security by using Tailscale and Cloudflare tunnels, as current Mac Mini hosting is insufficiently secure for web applications. The strategy for AI development involves using Claude Sonnet as the supervisor agent and Kimi K 2.5 for worker models to manage token consumption and optimize tasks.

Infrastructure and Future of AI
The conversation concluded with an emphasis on building robust AI infrastructure, including a custom CRM built into Robbothor to facilitate coordination among specialized agents. It was strongly advised that businesses must adopt AI infrastructure within the next two years to scale and achieve market dominance
decisions:
* Secure local application hosting process The process for securely hosting applications locally should utilize Tail Scale VPN coupled with Cloudflare Tunnels for access restriction and tunneling.
* Kimi K 2.5 assistant model selection Kimi K 2.5 should be utilized as the primary assistant agent model due to its efficiency and sufficient performance for non-coding tasks.
* Adopt supervisor and worker agent architecture The AI operational architecture should utilize a high-level supervisor agent to manage specialized worker agents, eliminating the need for direct communication between workers and the user.
* Claude Code remote session utility The Claude Code remote control feature should be utilized for managing remote work sessions, providing a secure alternative to working through Open Claw directly.
* AI business email handling system A system must be implemented for the AI (Robbothor) to handle all business emails, including responding to clients, logging details, and escalating issues to the user.
* Mac Mini server headless configuration The Mac Mini server must be configured to run headless (without a screen) and placed in a remote location once all system services and remote access are established.
nextSteps: Philip D'Agostino will send the GitHub link for Robothor to Eugene Ugolkov.
Eugene Ugolkov will install the new version of Claude to try the remote control feature and download the terminal app on his phone to use the remote control session.

1nWr-RZYza_HJqtGDvS675rmChZBk9xfUi18IAa0.title: Performance and Ideas Overview
1nWr-RZYza_HJqtGDvS675rmChZBk9xfUi18IAa0.summary:
ImpetusRX Platform Positive Feedback
The ImpetusRX platform received very positive user feedback, with some stating it could almost replace an EHR system, leading to a primary feature request for more extended functions related to labs and image screening.

AI R&D and Strategic Alignment
The company is expanding its AI R&D team to focus on building new applications, maintaining alignment with the strategy of emphasizing existing successful offerings over non-existent features.

Automating Outreach Using AI
Discussions centered on leveraging AI tools, specifically Claude, to automate tedious research tasks like scraping websites for pharmacy contact details to facilitate marketplace expansion and improve outreach efficiency.
decisions:
* Customer Needs Feedback Collection Process Feedback collection on customer needs will continue to be gathered by Paul and shared with Philip and Rashelle.
* R&D Team Application Development Process The R&D team will focus on building new applications, which will then be handed off to the rest of the team for maintenance and operation.
* Paul's Pharmacy Outreach Role Defined Paul will conduct initial outreach and demos for desired pharmacies to extend the marketplace, handing off qualified leads to Samantha to handle the commercial discussions.
nextSteps: Paul Martyniuk will work with Samantha on integrating pharmacies by doing initial outreach and demos for nice pharmacists that are in demand but not yet integrated, and then introducing them to Samantha to discuss the commercial part.
Philip D'Agostino will finish building the application for impetus one and then hand it off to Greg and Michelle and the rest of the team.
Philip D'Agostino will talk to Samantha about giving users an option to upgrade to impetus one for free and then eventually force them to migrate after about two months.

1SWItguJRMzJaOwwHcWXp0IZYRtxbuDx4INJFA3z.title: Thrive Rx Weekly Leadership Meeting
1SWItguJRMzJaOwwHcWXp0IZYRtxbuDx4INJFA3z.summary:
Financial setup completed with a significant wire transfer and the immediate purchase of critical equipment and the business strategy finalized

Funding and Account Setup
Shahad Abdolrazzak confirmed a $50,000 wire transfer to the Chase account today at 1:30 p.m. Central Time, dedicating $75,000 for the lifealyzer and clear room equipment. Samantha D'Agostino will start a QuickBooks account next week and link it to the Chase account for future accounting needs.

Equipment Procurement Decision
The team decided to order the lifealyzer machine today or Monday due to its long lead time and to purchase the $20,000 capsule machine as soon as possible. Acquisition of the capsule machine allows for immediate sales of supplements like NMN, providing profitability from day 1.

Initial Business Strategy
Partners agreed to immediately launch with a fulfillment center, supplements, and research chemicals before obtaining their license to capitalize on quick revenue opportunities like NMN. The preferred logo, consisting of 1 set of lettering, was finalized for use on the website and signage.


Rate this Summary: Helpful or Not Helpful
decisions:
* Lyophilizer and Clear Room Funding Available funds up to $75,000 will be used to purchase the lyophilizer machine and clear room, with plans to provide additional funding later if needed.
* Quickbooks and Chase Account Setup QuickBooks will be implemented next week, and the Chase account will be connected to start keeping financial records.
* Shahad Bank Signing Confirmation The banker (Barbara) will be contacted immediately to ensure Shahad can sign the Thrive account documents in person at the 1:30 PM Central time meeting today.
* $20,000 Capsules Machine Purchase The $20,000 capsules machine will be purchased immediately to enable selling supplements like NMN starting next week.
* Using 503B Pricing Strategy Cheap 503B pricing will be utilized through Thrive to quickly acquire clients and establish the business.
* Clean Room Deposit Payment Method Samantha will inquire whether online payment is an option for the 20% clean room deposit, rather than relying on a check or bank transfer process.
* Finalizing Selected Logo Design The preferred set of lettering for the logo will be finalized, and all necessary image files, including a vectorized image, will be secured.
* Eugene Legit Script Website Responsibility Eugene will take over the website development and handle all required tasks related to Conrad and the Legit Script certification process.
* Confirming On-Site Visit Dates Samantha will finalize the specific 2 to 4-day visit schedule between the 12th and the 24th now that there are no conflicting dates confirmed.
* Fulfillment Before License Strategy The business strategy is confirmed to start the fulfillment center, supplements production, and research chemicals sales immediately before obtaining the formal license.
* Determining Staffing Needs On-Site Staffing needs, including the type and number of staff, computers, and desks, will be determined during the upcoming visit.
nextSteps: Shahad Abdolrazzak will wire $50,000 today at 1:30 p.m. Central time and ensure a smooth signing process at the Chase Bank meeting in Houston.
Samantha D'Agostino will start a Quickbooks account next week, connect the Chase account to it for accounting, and manually input past expenses.

Samantha D'Agostino will email Barbara to confirm Shahad Abdolrazzak's 1:30 p.m. signing appointment at Chase and will call Barbara after the meeting to notify her that Shahad Abdolrazzak will also be calling her.
Samantha D'Agostino will forward all emails from the clear room vendor and ask if there is an option to pay the deposit online.
Samantha D'Agostino will send the selected logo to be finalized and obtain all necessary image files, including a vectorized image.
Samantha D'Agostino will finalize the two to four-day visit dates between the 12th and the 24th.
The group will order the lifalizer machine and the clear room deposit today or Monday using the $75,000 for funding, and will also purchase the $20,000 capsule machine as soon as possible.
The group will discuss the need for refrigerators and freezers and plan staffing needs (number of staff, computers, and desks) during the visit.

Team updates covered ongoing development efforts website cost reduction and critical production bug troubleshooting with Elguja Nemsadze focusing on e-commerce API development before a planned vacation

Cost Reduction and System Updates
Danylo Boiko updated the liquid website to decrease server loads and costs by moving the Iron Sale pharma website off the VP Engine platform. They also discovered and plan to revise many unused, paid-for sites in the web engine account.

E-commerce and Medusa Integration
Elguja Nemsadze is developing custom Medusa registration and authentication logic, as the desired email-based login is not supported out of the box, estimating 1 week for initial completion including testing. The implementation of Dispense Pro configuration in staging is complete, with only pharmacy configuration needed for task finalization.

Production Bug and Data Migration
A critical free form prescribing issue persists in production despite a fix in staging, with Illia Tutko and Elguja Nemsadze debugging the 'not found' error likely caused by a wrong session token. Gregory Popov is building a custom solution to integrate Authorized.net payments while migrating payment and subscription data from WordPress to Medusa.

* Twilio/HubSpot ticket delay Ticket 1286 regarding the HubSpot Twilio subscription will not be assigned immediately to prevent the team from getting sidetracked from higher priority tasks.

18DQyvXQCDv0KbePrMZDPXUyjhfwlEK_mmEK0sjo.title: Weekly Alignment Meetings
18DQyvXQCDv0KbePrMZDPXUyjhfwlEK_mmEK0sjo.summary:
Business travel updates covered, a new pharmacy venture announced, and a development process improvement plan approved, featuring increased resource commitment for agile implementation and AI integration.

New Pharmacy Venture Launched
Philip D'Agostino announced the launch of a new pharmacy in Houston called Thrive RX, which will incorporate automation like a capsule machine and a lyophilizer. The venture is a partnership with the CEO of Kaduceus, indicating long-term aspirations beyond a standard pharmacy.

Agile Process Implementation Approved
Craig Nicholson presented a plan to address development cadence issues by shifting to a 2-week sprint cycle with structured ceremonies like story pointing. The plan was approved, requiring Craig Nicholson to increase their time commitment to 16 to 20 hours per week for 2 to 3 months to implement a fully operational mature agile process.

Developer Productivity and Strategy
Analysis showed that engineers Greg and Elguja were working, but their productivity was hampered by a lack of a mature agile process. Implementing the new agile process with story pointing will objectively track productivity and identify who is contributing by monitoring assigned story points versus completion.
decisions:
* Two-Month Operational Implementation Plan Approved An aggressive two-to-three month plan is approved, which includes implementing a mature agile process with story pointing, transitioning from one-week to two-week sprints, establishing Impetus One as the R&D testbed, rolling out the AI strategy, and increasing Craig Nicholson's time commitment from eight hours to 16-20 hours per week.
* Production Team Performance Measured by Agile The implementation of the new agile process will be used to objectively measure productivity across the entire production team, rather than immediately terminating Greg and Guja.
* Agentic Agile Development Standard Set The new agile process must specifically be an agentic (AI-driven) agile development process to remain competitive and meet the company's vision.
* Adan Collaboration on Impetus One Timelines Craig Nicholson will actively collaborate with Adan to build the relationship, focus on Impetus One timelines, and manage the process of converting R&D concepts into production products.
nextSteps: [Craig Nicholson] Discuss Strategy: Communicate plan strategy to Daniel and Samantha. Discuss required increase in weekly hours to 16 to 20 and adjust invoicing.
[Craig Nicholson] Increase Coaching: Increase dedicated time spent with Greg and Guja. Maintain motivation; track progress on implementation plan.
[Craig Nicholson] Meet Adan: Schedule 1.5 hours with Adan. Review current status of Impetus One development and 1 April deadline.

1H_RzgVFRd8CiBTnIizr0qdFT0c9WGOfDZU-JgPT.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
1H_RzgVFRd8CiBTnIizr0qdFT0c9WGOfDZU-JgPT.summary:
Workflow improvements and development progress were discussed with concerns regarding resource allocation for Valhalla Vitality and the necessity of addressing employee performance issues.

AI Driven Development Progress
Eugene Ugolkov demonstrated an AI-created website audit tool and reported significant progress using Claude for rapid prototyping of a new SEO module for Cattix. Philip D'Agostino requested an MCP for the audit tool to integrate it into the Robothor AI agent for experimentation and collaboration.

Resource Allocation and Performance
Frustration was expressed regarding the delayed pharma product, with resources being diverted from the main money-maker to Valhalla Vitality. Discussions focused on the unacceptable performance and lack of adherence to strategy by employees, resulting in the decision to replace a developer.

Agent Marketplace and Future Vision
The ultimate goal involves creating a single SEO management dashboard and an agent marketplace for storing and sharing custom AI agents and workflows. A major focus is on replacing traditional middle and lower-lane workers with AI and ensuring the stability of the core pharma business.
decisions:
* SEO Module Master Control Plan The new SEO module prototype requires Eugene to create a Master Control Plan (MCP) so that it can be integrated and experimented with using RoboThor.
* Tailscale Configuration Support Request Danylo will be contacted by Eugene to assist with configuring Tailscale on Windows due to ongoing connection issues.
* Greg’s Replacement Process Initiated The process to replace Greg has been initiated by Philip, with an expected timeline of two months required for hiring and onboarding a suitable candidate.
* Mikola Firing Within Two Weeks Mikola will be fired from the team within the next two weeks because of non-delivery and poor team collaboration.
* Cattix Integration with Robbothor Cattix will be registered as a tool for Robothor agents to utilize, enabling tasks such as weekly report generation based on performance data.
* Agent Configuration Repository Development A repository/hub for storing, sharing, and versioning agent configurations and workflows will be built, using GitHub as its foundation, with potential future development into a chargeable marketplace.
* VP Swing credentials sharing VP Swing credentials will be sent to Eugene so support can be contacted regarding the active license issue.
* VP Swing license discussion meeting Philip will join the call with Android and Zoriana/Eugene to discuss the VP Swing license issue.
nextSteps: [Eugene Ugolkov] Create MCP: Generate the MCP document for the module prototype.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Share Technical Files: Send the ARMD and architecture text file to Philip D'Agostino.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Contact Danylo: Message Danillo about configuration issues with Tailscale on Windows.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Get Staging Link: Ask Artem for the Valhalla Vitality Next staging site link.
[Philip D'Agostino] Build Repository: Create agent space hub repository similar to GitHub for configurations, workflows.
[Philip D'Agostino] Develop Onboarding: Build the Robothor onboarding agent configuration.
[Philip D'Agostino] Update Credentials: Change VP Swing password. Send updated credentials to Eugene Ugolkov.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Forward Issue: Forward issue details and VP Swing credentials to Zoriana

1FXFKm16-5m1i6LUV8nOxR-eVdNDCVFrj-q-VAXF.summary:
ImpetusRX database migration requires application updates while Hallandale ticket requires immediate code review with Brooksville deployment on hold for configuration details and the Marketplace for Business Unit nearing completion for staging.

Database Migration and Sandboxes
ImpetusRX database migration requires application compatibility updates due to potential issues with the newer MySQL version. Migration and sandbox setup for Authorize.net continued, with a focus on a test run in the stage environment.

Urgent Hallandale Ticket Review
Hallandale ticket work is complete and requires an urgent code review by Danylo Boiko before deployment to staging. Brooksville configuration is blocked because Illia Tutko was unaware of the required 2-part production setup process.

Marketplace and Bug Fixes
The Marketplace for Business Unit solution is nearing completion and will be ready for staging testing with approval from pharma teams already received. Urgent bug fixes are underway for a rewards and points plugin on Valhalla Vitality and a data-related error on the Practisync patient profile page.
decisions:
* Peaks project internal visibility ticket A small reminder ticket for the external Peaks project is created by Paul solely for visibility purposes, acknowledging it is outside the main platform.
* Hallandale ticket review and testing The Hallandale code review will be completed by Danylo, and Rochelle will perform the staging testing immediately afterward due to staffing absences.
* Illia reviews RXC API documentation Illia's next task, after completing the Hallandale ticket, is to review the RXC API documentation, which Rochelle will ensure is attached to the corresponding ticket.
* Marketplace business unit deployment requirement All ready business units and the “enable SQ” items must be deployed simultaneously to production.
nextSteps: Conduct testing on staging for Hallandale and the Marketplace project while Rizzi is absent.
Share RXC documentation with Illia.
Perform code review for Illia's Hallandale ticket.
Assist Gregory with scaffolding for the migration test run.
Coordinate with Danylo for the migration dry run on staging.
Address the Authorize.net sandbox account issues.
Continue focus on the Peaks project integration.
Ping Rochelle once the Hallandale ticket is pushed to staging.
Review the RXC API documentation and credentials.
Add descriptions and context to the current bug tickets for visibility.
Fix the PracticeSync patient profile access error.
Review Farhan’s work from yesterday.
Create a "visibility ticket" for Gregory's work on the Peaks project.
Update the Hallandale/RXC ticket with API documentation and credentials.
Coordinate a potential meeting with Webugol and Rochelle.

1WuPWM-z4eyXecsDyfkICrZzPl2_PQEfGP5VD9R3.summary:
Impetus database monitoring and Pharmacy API staging deployments were completed, with Brooksville, South Lake, and Shields pharmacies configured for testing.

Impetus and Live File Updates
The Impetus database and application preparation included patching incompatible queries and the production environment is now running without issues. LifeFile update changes for the Pharmacy API and ImpetusRX were released to staging and additional pharmacy configurations were completed.

Pharmacy API Validation and Testing
Illia Tutko's pull request for the Pharmacy API requires manual validation by Elguja Nemsadze before production approval for Hallandale, with a performance concern noted for Hallandale's prescription sending. Brooksville, South Lake, and Shields credentials were added to staging, ready for testing by Rizzi Ann Gorospe.

Workflows and E-commerce Preparation
E-commerce API plans are ready, but a manual provider must be built to support generating 6 digits per out request. Workflows are almost complete, and non-Medusa dependent workflows are planned for deployment to production today, following successful testing of simple workflows.
decisions:
* BU video deployment plan The plan to deploy the BU video (or related items) to production is set for tomorrow.
* Share API document link The public API document link will be shared with a potential client for assessment purposes, though they will not be able to use it without sandbox credentials.
* Investigate API performance issue A ticket will be created to find and resolve the 32-second performance delay observed when sending prescriptions via United Pharma API.

1HAsoUzrf43xJ1yzKzyBvPZqZJD90-7v9-OUho78.title: Debrief & Planning Meeting: AI Session & Future Curriculum
1HAsoUzrf43xJ1yzKzyBvPZqZJD90-7v9-OUho78.summary:
AI session feedback informed future training structure with beginner and advanced sessions and mandated employee quizzes for adoption.

Address Service Account Issue
An active note-taking service account, believed to be canceled, was identified with a last login in August. It was decided to ensure the associated credit card is blocked to prevent further charges for the unwanted account.

Review AI Training Feedback
Feedback from 5 attendees showed decent results regarding session clarity and comfort with AI tools. Attendees requested that future sessions be beginner-level walkthroughs and strongly desired written guides following the training.

Mandated AI Training Structure
The company will alternate beginner and advanced AI sessions, with the advanced track requiring application and approval for exclusivity. Beginner sessions must be held biweekly, implement quizzes produced by Gemini, and participants must pass to avoid remediation and potential future termination.


Rate this Summary: Helpful or Not Helpful
nextSteps: [Sam] Block Charge: Ensure cancellation and blocking of the credit card charge for the notetaker account.
[Sandra Zelaya] Generate Guides: Produce the guide for the last session using Notebook LM; refine using Gemini to create beginner and advanced versions.
[Philip D'Agostino] Schedule Advanced: Start hosting advanced sessions requiring applicant registration and entry approval.
[The group] Maintain Schedule: Continue beginner AI sessions on at least a bi-weekly cadence.
[The group] Implement Grading: Roll out quizzes and tests, establishing required time limits and passing standards.
[Sandra Zelaya, Jennifer Guerrero] Produce Quizzes: Create AI training quizzes using Gemini based on session content.
[Jennifer Guerrero, Sandra Zelaya] Consult Leadership: Consult with Samantha and Daniel regarding the implementation of the AI training plan rollout.

1XgyB5vRtKuEUuVDAP7HJ79HMqN6UdZSVBrJmrQ3.title: 30 min with Philip (Gary Poole)
1XgyB5vRtKuEUuVDAP7HJ79HMqN6UdZSVBrJmrQ3.summary:
Agentic AI research and Robothor's escalation were discussed with Ironsail Pharma's platform for scaling operations.

Agentic AI Research Discussed
The meeting began with a discussion on the need for deep research into best practices for Agentic AI for March 2026. A key question was raised about how the AI agent, Robothor, escalates notifications to users who rely solely on its system.

Platform Pain Points Reviewed
The current platform, Blendare, was criticized for forcing associated pharmacies to purchase its software to view orders, which is considered an unreasonable ask. The core problem is the difficulty of logging into 4 to 6 different pharmacy portals for multiple pharmacy operations.

Impetus RX Platform Features
Philip D'Agostino detailed the Impetus RX platform's ability to handle custom pricing, wholesale bulk orders, and EMR functionality. A customized project including CRM, sales, and payments was estimated at approximately $15,000 with a 6-week delivery timeline.
decisions:
* Pricing Configuration Set by Organization The platform is configured to allow the organization (Gary’s company) to set its own pricing using defaults per medication, markups per pharmacy, and overriding prices for specific business units.
* Zero Cost Pharmacy Integration Standard No cost is charged to pharmacies for onboarding or integrating with the platform for managing client orders.
* Automatic Invoice and Credit Card Charging Process The platform will automatically generate an invoice and allow staff to finalize charging the client's credit card on file after an order is placed.
* Custom Software Development Budget and Timeline Custom software development for a simplified CRM, Sales, and Payments platform is budgeted at $15,000 with an estimated completion time of six weeks.
* Platform Hosting Service Cost Hosting services for the new platform are available for $1,000 per month.
nextSteps: [The group] Research AI: Look into best practices for Agentic AI build in March 2026. Conduct deep research to optimize the current plan.
[Gary Poole] Discuss Software: Talk to business partner Naveiv about the platform possibilities and current offerings.
[Gary Poole] Share Contact: Send cell phone number and contact details to Philip D'Agostino.

1L094nrXR9J34v2_iN7wNeR-wUHuWRRUwMbBleyf.title: Robothor Walkthrough
1L094nrXR9J34v2_iN7wNeR-wUHuWRRUwMbBleyf.summary:
Robothor's agentic platform leverages Python for coordinated operations via a centralized dashboard, with a long-term goal of AI-first business applications and workflow monetization.

Robothor Platform Architecture
Robothor, an internal company assistant, utilizes agentic automations within a framework featuring an agentic engine and memory system to centralize operational coordination. The core components include a CLI, Telegram connection, the Helm channel, and an onboarding agent, with PostgreSQL handling the memory system.

Prototype Status and Vision
The current system is a slow prototype due to model limitations, but the goal is to reach an MVP by January to reliably transfer and trigger automations from other platforms. A primary focus is building an agent hub on programmaticresources.com to sell specialized agentic workflows for business operations.

Addressing Stability and Security
A key system component must convert successful agentic workflows into stable, cost-effective strict code automations to prevent instability issues caused by LLMs. Necessary guardrails must be implemented before and after the agent to address significant security challenges like prompt injection.
nextSteps: [Philip D'Agostino] Test N8N: Experiment with N8N integration using the existing MCP; verify functionality.
[Philip D'Agostino] Set up Demo: Duplicate the Robothor instance and configure server permissions for company access via Tailscale.
[Philip D'Agostino] Local Setup: Assist colleagues in setting up their local Robothor installations to gather troubleshooting feedback.
[Philip D'Agostino] Security Plan: Improve the prompt injection defense plan; push tested fixes to Git repository.
[Philip D'Agostino] Share Repository: Provide the GitHub repository URL to the team for access and potential contributions.

1yHeMrRZJvl641LJgfvBNqSjc_4XK7e_hOHkSD9D.title: Monthly Business Review
1yHeMrRZJvl641LJgfvBNqSjc_4XK7e_hOHkSD9D.summary:
Data analysis utilized a 28-day comparison for February performance, revealing growth in net sales but a concerning drop in new client acquisition, necessitating an overhaul of the affiliate program strategy.

Comparative Data and Sales Growth
Comparative data analysis was set to compare the last 28 days of February versus January, confirming growth in net sales, gross revenue, and total orders. Average Order Value also grew slightly, which is a positive outcome for promotional months.

New Client Acquisition Drop
New client acquisition decreased month-over-month, highlighting the need for enhancements in the client acquisition channel. The blended cost of acquisition grew to around 150, which is higher than the target cost of 100.

Affiliate Program Structural Issue
The affiliate channel had minimal revenue, with only 5 affiliates delivering 80% of results, pointing to a structural problem with the current affiliate strategy. A decision was made to address the affiliate program's performance suggesting a potential structural problem that needs a detailed, separate discussion.


Rate this Summary: Helpful or Not Helpful
decisions:
* Metric comparison period set Monthly metrics comparison periods are standardized to 28 days versus the corresponding 28 days of the previous month.
* Affiliate payouts accounting process Affiliate costs related to February efforts must be accurately calculated and assigned to February for performance and cash flow tracking, even if the payment occurs in a subsequent month.
* Affiliate signing tracking process The Sales team must notify Samantha via chat immediately when new affiliates are signed, specifying which pharmacy the affiliate is linked to, to ensure a smoother process.
nextSteps: [Olesia Astakhova] Update Affiliate Numbers: Add accurate payout amounts to used affiliate statistics.
[Olesia Astakhova] Separate Invoices: Separate February affiliate invoices paid in April. Ensure accurate cash flow tracking by month.

[Olesia Astakhova] Execute Affiliate Playbook: Implement affiliate playbook to improve accountability. Terminate non-compliant contracts immediately if affiliates fail to post required content or bring clients within 2 months.
[Olesia Astakhova] Reshape Product Bundles: Continue work on product bundle and category restructure plan. Seek required management approval before implementation.
[Samantha D'Agostino] Review Campaign Idea: Review the sales campaign idea Olesia shared in Google Chat.
[Rosanna Paknoush] Notify New Affiliate Info: Notify Samantha DAgostino when signing new affiliates. Share the new affiliate name and assigned pharmacy location.

1hn0IBM46_RpmVNMvBC0hVrNOG1CbeeM143ydtAX.title: Ironsail x Webugol Internal Marketing
1hn0IBM46_RpmVNMvBC0hVrNOG1CbeeM143ydtAX.summary:
Thrive pharmacy development preparation and team role clarification led to major AI investment plans for enhanced financial management and local SEO.

Thrive Pharmacy Development Update
The Thrive pharmacy is in a bare-bones operational stage while awaiting a lyophilizer and a capsule maker, with manufacturing expected to launch closer to the summer. The team clarified roles, deciding against retaining Greg while agreeing to keep Elguja for the Medusa project due to their agreeable nature and problem-solving focus.

Advanced AI Model Implementation
Claude code is preferred over Open Claw for project building, having successfully gathered and filtered property addresses for a client's direct mail campaign. Mikola, a developer, was terminated and will work for 2 more weeks while a replacement is sought.

Major AI Infrastructure Investment
Philip D'Agostino revealed plans to purchase 4 Mac Studios to run trillion-parameter open-source models, estimating the cost at approximately $48,000. This investment will support building a financial bot using Claude to handle financial reporting and a local SEO review replier module to fix Valhalla Vitality's low 3.8 Google Maps rating.
decisions:
* Keep Elguja for Medusa Team Alusa will be kept on the Medusa team, as decided by Philip, who will try to make him feel accepted.
* Valhalla Vitality Google Rating Goal The Valhalla Vitality Google Maps review rating goal is set to 4.3, requiring action to deal with the existing bad reviews.
* Automate Financial Billing Operations The billing operation workflow, currently tracked via spreadsheets, will be converted into an automated database and web application using Claude, which will then be plugged into Robothor.
* Purchase Mac Studio M5 Ultra Multiple Mac Studio M5 Ultra machines will be purchased upon release (around June) to run open-source LLMs locally for improved productivity and cost efficiency.
nextSteps: [Philip D'Agostino] Retain Elguja: Attempt retaining Elguja on Medusa project team.
[Eugene Ugolkov, Arnold] Support Elguja: Show support to Elguja making him feel accepted by the team.
[Philip D'Agostino, Caroline] Build Finance App: Develop a financial application/database system utilizing existing billing spreadsheets.
[Philip D'Agostino] Purchase Macs: Buy multiple Mac Studio M5 Ultra machines when they are released.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Configure Macs: Purchase and configure Mac Studio M5 Ultra devices with Philip.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Test Robothor Agent: Test the newly updated Robothor onboarding agent functionality tomorrow.

1zlHYEbD1FdNDQFOqK0nyIObk0Vxci31bpUk6H5D.title: Demo -- Boladji Agueh / Squadra Solutions
1zlHYEbD1FdNDQFOqK0nyIObk0Vxci31bpUk6H5D.summary:
Meeting covered privacy and security concerns in a functional agent system with discussion of current architecture and future goals.

Addressing System Security and Privacy
Privacy concerns are mitigated through correct API key configuration and local system setup on a standalone Nvidia Stack Lenovo PGX Think Station, secured via SSH and Cloudflare tunnels. While many security issues are mitigated with Cloudflare and Tailscale, prompt injection and user permissions remain challenging to address.

System Architecture and Agent Workflow
The system architecture features a supervisor agent that interacts with the user, with 15 isolated agents running beneath it that manage workflows via Python scripts and YAML configuration. A core PostgreSQL memory database and an embedding RAG memory system are used for indexing long-term searchable data on the PGX platform.

Development Approach and Future Vision
The system, currently a prototype, is built on Python scripts, crons, and webhooks, featuring an agent that performs nightly self-improvement by logging failures and submitting code requests. The goal is to achieve a production-ready MVP by January 1, 2027, with email management identified as the top game-changing use case.


Rate this Summary: Helpful or Not Helpful




Next steps
* [Boladji A.] Review Readme: Study documentation to understand the system setup.
* [Boladji A.] Compare Atlas: Use Claude to compare project PRD against existing architecture. Determine required roadmap steps and scope for implementation.
* [Boladji A.] Share Architecture: Send requested architecture file to Philip D'Agostino.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Review Architecture: Examine architectural recommendations provided by Boladji A. Give feedback and validate the approach.
* [Boladji A.] Download Code: Access GitHub repository and download source code and necessary configuration files. Begin system testing.
* [Boladji A.] Setup System: Enlist 17-year-old child to assist with the deployment and configuration of the platform.

1vOCPn0lst-bDXBy5L6NzHPCCbl3VAbOPcv8fEpw.title: Sprint Planning
1vOCPn0lst-bDXBy5L6NzHPCCbl3VAbOPcv8fEpw.summary:
Meeting covered e-commerce authentication flow, N8N workflow deployment, and critical resolution of a false order success status.

E-commerce and Hubspot Updates
The simple e-commerce authentication flow is established, though deeper testing is required for the complete profile and order flow. Gregory Popov needs to confirm who will handle the integration with Hubspot for sending mobile messages.

Hallandale and API Priorities
The Hallandale release included signature and credential configuration, and the top priority remains completing API integrations for all LifeFile pharmacies. A critical issue with Hallandale orders showing a false 'received' status was identified and requires a fix to pass integration failure messages back to Impetus RX.

ScriptSure Issue and Work Migration
Elguja Nemsadze was temporarily reassigned 100% to resolve a ScriptSure issue in Impetus One, pausing all current e-commerce work. Farhan Sheikh and Nadjib Boumekhiet successfully deployed N8N workflows to production as part of the Zapier migration effort.
decisions:
* Monday deployment policy confirmed A no-Friday deployment policy is enforced, requiring ready items to be deployed on Monday, unless specific deadlines (like Hallandale) necessitate immediate delivery.
* Tracking deployed workflow document Farhan must share the spreadsheet used to track deployed workflows from Zapier to N8N with Paul.
* Error visibility for failed orders Ilia must create a new ticket to test and integrate Elguja's existing Pull Request (PR) to ensure orders that receive a 200 OK status from the pharmacy API but subsequently fail integration are changed from 'Received' to 'Failed' status in Impetus RX, displaying the reason for failure.
* E-commerce data migration priority Data migration is established as the first priority for the e-commerce platform work.
* Elguja allocated to Impetus One ScriptSure Elguja must pause his e-commerce work and allocate 100% of his time to resolving the 'ScriptSure' functionality issue in Impetus One, working with Adan.
* Gregory work handover to Elguja Gregory will hand over all relevant work and knowledge, including the Hopspot integration, to Elguja.
nextSteps: [Elguja Nemsadze] Share Documentation: Share Swagger documentation detailing e-commerce endpoints with the frontend team.
[Farhan Sheikh] Share Tracking: Share spreadsheet tracking deployed N8N workflows with Paul Edward Borja.
[Illia Tutko] Release Migration: Deploy migration for removing CDS field from Hallandale production environment.
[Illia Tutko] Deploy Brooksville: Release Brooksville live file configuration to production environment.
[Rochelle Blaza] Create Ticket: Create ticket for Illia concerning integration error visibility; ensure failure messages pass back to Impetus RX.
[Elguja Nemsadze] Share PR: Share PR link related to checking integration response statuses with Illia and Paul Edward Borja.
[Illia Tutko] Test Error PR: Test Elguja Nemsadzes PR for handling non-200 integration responses and fix necessary issues.
[Rochelle Blaza] Schedule Meeting: Schedule follow-up meeting, potentially Monday, to discuss new e-commerce tickets.
[Gregory Popov] Handoff Work: Handoff all current responsibilities and related knowledge to Elguja Nemsadze.

1RaZ1GMa_Ts0jD_CrIERfn5fy5B5yjaV5dB4N01m.title: Thrive Rx Weekly Leadership Meeting
1RaZ1GMa_Ts0jD_CrIERfn5fy5B5yjaV5dB4N01m.summary:
Scheduling Philip D'Agostino's 3-day visit, financial updates on the clean room and lyophilizer, and developing an operations launch strategy for the fulfillment center.

Reviewing Visit Schedule and Logistics
Philip D'Agostino's visit is confirmed for Wednesday through Friday next week, with a full day dedicated to Thrive and one to Kaducas for workflow management. The focus of the visit is discussing logistics, including setting up offices and computers.

Addressing Financial and Equipment Status
An outstanding balance of $8,125 is due for the lyophilizer, and a 60% deposit has been paid for the clean room, with delivery expected around April 10th. Given vendor history, the clean room's operational readiness is optimistically anticipated by June.

Operations Launch Strategy and Staffing
The goal is to launch operations by April 1st, starting with liquids if the capsule machine is delayed, with a minimum staffing requirement of 2 people. The lyophilizer does not need to be in the sterile room because the process sterilizes the vials, allowing for dry storage of peptide products.


Rate this Summary: Helpful or Not Helpful
decisions:
* 503 Success Portal Review The "503 success" portal will be reviewed during the upcoming visit to determine ease of integration for the order fulfillment system.
* Initial Equipment Acquisition Plan Initial equipment acquisition will include 1-2 desks and 1-2 refrigerators, followed by shipping computers and monitors once the desks are installed.
* First Day Operations Checklist A complete checklist defining all necessary resources (desks, computers, refrigerators, and lyophilizer) required for the first day of operations will be developed during next week's meeting.
* Website Built Using AI The company website will be built using AI by Philip within one week following the definitive discussion on branding and offerings.
nextSteps: [Philip D'Agostino] Build Website: Develop the company website quickly and efficiently. Utilize AI tools for rapid construction within 1 week.
[Philip D'Agostino] Review Portal: Examine the 503 success portal documentation. Determine ease of integration with planned systems.
[Shahad Abdolrazzak] Create Equipment List: Generate a comprehensive list of all required supplies and equipment. Include necessary freezers and refrigerators.
[Philip D'Agostino] Ship Computers: Ship computers and monitors down to the office location. Perform this action after desks are secured.

1q_9JdpR2FUjBZK0wSvOaE487q3iIqKL2ZdkxzCM.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1q_9JdpR2FUjBZK0wSvOaE487q3iIqKL2ZdkxzCM.summary:
Backend development focused on S3 and Loki configuration with Medusa requiring external validation for input data and a revised registration workflow confirmed for stakeholders.

Backend Configuration and Dependencies
Backend development paused database migration to configure S3 storage for Medusa and upgrade the Loki monitoring tool, anticipating S3 readiness this week. Integration tasks are waiting on the S3 configuration and the necessary production credentials for pharmacy deployment.

Medusa E-commerce Validation Needs
Medusa requires external validation for input data like phone numbers and lacks built-in ordering process validation, necessitating additional development work. Integrating the OTP module requires the HubSpot endpoint to send emails and SMS messages to registered customers.

User Sign-in and Registration Workflow
The password field will be replaced with a 6-digit one-time code sent via email or SMS for existing users post-migration to sign in. The confirmed registration workflow for stakeholders requires users to enter their email, receive a code, and then proceed to a second page for personal details.
decisions:
* LifeFile webhook payload fix The incompatible LifeFile webhook payload must be fixed internally, and a ticket will be created for assignment to address this issue.
* Implement mock logic for pharmacy webhooks Mock logic must be created for every pharmacy enabled in the sandbox environment to simulate webhooks, addressing limitations where external pharmacy sandboxes do not support them.
* Medusa account access flow defined The Medusa login flow will use a one-time code for existing users, replacing the password field, and the registration flow will maintain a two-step process: (1) email/phone verification via OTP, followed by (2) collection of required personal details (first name, last name, date of birth, state, phone number, and gender).
nextSteps: [Danylo Boiko] Configure S3: Work on S3 storage configuration for Medusa e-commerce. Finish monitoring tool Loki upgrade.
[Elguja Nemsadze] Validate Ordering: Work on ordering data validation within Medusa.
[Elguja Nemsadze, Gregory Popov] Sync Endpoints: Discuss Medusa endpoint requirements for sharing with Eugene's team.
[Paul Edward Borja] Create Webhook Ticket: Create fresh ticket based on Elguja's fix description for the LifeFile webhook issue.
[Paul Edward Borja] Contact Jhon: Reach out to Jhon Ray Angcon to discuss current work items.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Dosing Guideline: Work on the dosing guideline task.
[The group] Request Credentials: Ask for production credentials for pharmacy configuration due to missing documentation details.
[Illia Tutko] Deploy Pharmacies: Deploy Brooksville and Shields pharmacies to the environment.
[Illia Tutko] Fix Signature Bug: Take ticket regarding signature field bug.
[Illia Tutko] Display Prescription Error: Address task to display prescription error messages to providers.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Integrate OK Capsule API: Work on OK Capsule API integration with Medusa.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Assist Migration: Continue helping Farhan with N8N migration tasks.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Update Farhan Status: Check the status of Farhan's deployed workflows and update relevant tickets with details.
[Rochelle Blaza] Provide Client Data: Provide new client pharmacy information, including practice name and clinic name.

1-m6750n3SHmsCy144Wsukvc63q-ccJRQJIEayUk.summary:
The session centered on regulatory compliance, and a growth strategy for blog reinstatement, with a key decision for profitability analysis.

Peptide Content Strategy
Discussions with Legit Script regarding peptide policy are ongoing, but the company is credentialed by the NABP. The group agreed to allow Rosanna Paknoush to discuss specific peptides like BPC and AOD on TikTok as an educational piece, avoiding direct company tags.

Blog Reinstatement and Risk
A strategy was set to reinstate 240 drafted blogs over 18 to 20 days, which previously generated thousands of monthly traffic visits. The risk of a LegitScript warning is deemed small compared to the potential benefits, with the safety of a recently renewed Legit Script status.
nextSteps: [Konrad Arciszewski] Secure Payment Backup: Identify backup payment processors beyond Zen Payments and NMI to mitigate risks if the primary processor is shut down.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Restore Content: Update and recreate necessary blogs to meet current SEO requirements; modify internal links within blogs to exclude product pages.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Develop New Content: Start creating new peptide content to cover previously untouched keywords following the blog restoration period.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Prepare Peptide Ads: Build and stage specific advertising campaigns for peptides, ready to launch immediately upon FDA regulatory announcement.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Retarget Past Clients: Prepare email campaigns or sequences targeting the existing large database of former peptide patients to restore client engagement.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Analyze Peptide Costs: Collect current market statistics and forecast cost per acquisition for individual peptides.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Prioritize Campaigns: Compare peptide cost per acquisition data with current margins to determine launch priority for ad campaigns.
[Olesia Astakhova] Document Execution Plan: Outline the discussed three-phase peptide execution plan; send the document summary to Philip and Samantha for official approval.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Consult Philip: Discuss the execution plan, including Legit Script compliance, with Philip tomorrow to gain necessary approval.
[Konrad Arciszewski] Align Olesia: Touch base with Olesia Astakhova to ensure she is fully aligned and loop closed regarding the peptide execution plan.

1KLEu-YCw8EEr6g-aS5Fr5wAm9HSWx9U3bhvRMyq.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1KLEu-YCw8EEr6g-aS5Fr5wAm9HSWx9U3bhvRMyq.summary:
Database migration faced date field issues, with e-commerce dry migration showing clean data, and payment integration focusing on authorize.net and API accounts.

Database Migration and Alerts
Impetus staging database migration encountered issues with date fields showing as 0s, while a WordPress fix was confirmed and alerts were upgraded for Rochelle Blaza and Rizzi Ann Gorospe. The alert system upgrade required prompt handling of all alerts by the recipients.

E-commerce and Payment Updates
E-commerce migration involved a dry run for the last 100 customers, with production data appearing clean, requiring validation of Gregory Popov’s changes to ensure customer log-in. Gregory Popov is focusing on authorize.net payments, including setting up an API for a potential cash account and reviewing credit card purchasing flow.

CDS Validation and Webhooks
The Clinical Difference Statement (CDS) validation issue caused script sending problems because the field was incorrectly required, necessitating the removal of the requirement for non-GLP1 medications. Illia Tutko finished the new live file webhooks ticket 1325 and planned staging deployment while also addressing a request to remove prescription rejection error validation.
nextSteps: [Danylo Boiko] Fix Database: Resolve date field issues where values are zero during Impetus staging database migration to Amazon.
[Elguja Nemsadze, Gregory Popov] Validate E-commerce: Catch up to validate e-commerce migration scripts and changes compatibility, ensuring customer login functionality.
[Gregory Popov] Prepare API: Prepare API for the web team covering authorized net payments cash account scenario.
[Gregory Popov] Confirm Deployment: Confirm ready for deployment items with Elguja to initiate subsequent deployment process.
[Paul Edward Borja] Access Followup: Follow up with Olesia from the other team regarding HubSpot access availability.
[Illia Tutko] Deploy Webhooks: Deploy new live file webhooks (ticket 1325) to the staging environment.
[Illia Tutko] Remove Validation: Remove validation requiring Clinical Difference Statement (CDS) field input in prescription form.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Create Tests: Create automated tests for ready for deployment items before submitting the Pull Request.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Tag Tester: Tag Rizzi Ann Gorospe in tickets requiring Quality Assurance testing.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Test Deployment: Test ready for deployment items currently in production and move associated tickets to done status.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Report Medusa: Report specific Medusa web hook delivery issue details to Danylo and create a corresponding tracking ticket.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Export Patients: Export patients from PracticeSync based on the ticket assigned by Rochelle.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] File Ticket: File a ticket for the South Lake pending order status issue for easier tracking.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Check Priority: Back read and check the priority list provided by Rochelle.
[Rochelle Blaza] Verify CDS: Follow up with the Pharma team to confirm if the Clinical Difference Statement value is correct and valid.

10JLbtX__zr3VVYX0cQw78dkC4uYsWhV9ORIziGx.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
10JLbtX__zr3VVYX0cQw78dkC4uYsWhV9ORIziGx.summary:
New customer onboarding decline prompted an SEO blog revival strategy, countered by concerns over LegitScript status and website speed issues, while confirming 74000 contacts migrated to HubSpot and Medusa integration progress.

Onboarding Decline and Affiliates
New customer onboardings dropped 25% month-to-month, which was attributed to decreased brand searches and poor performance from the influencer/affiliate program. It was confirmed that the decline in brand searches is strongly linked to the reduced effectiveness of influencer marketing due to public distrust and AI content proliferation.

Peptide Blog Relaunch Strategy
A strategy was set to counteract the affiliate decline by reviving approximately 388 old peptide blogs through AI rewriting for enhanced SEO performance. Concern arose that restoring old content and backlinks could jeopardize existing LegitScript approval, though it was assured this would only minimally affect certain Google ads.

Migration Status and Technology Future
The HubSpot migration is near completion with 74000 contacts synchronized, while Medusa implementation is proceeding despite integration hurdles. The new website must prioritize speed and clarity, eliminating the 'Astrid and Thoryn' AI cartoon branding, with a firm commitment to future development using Next/React technologies.
nextSteps: [Elena] Plan Peptides: Prepare plan for reviving peptide blog pages; include necessary changes for proper function.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Check Onboarding: Review month-to-month new customer onboarding figures.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Initiate Blog Work: Ask team to begin preparatory work for rewriting and publishing 200+ blogs.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Launch Test Campaign: Execute test Phentermine campaign using personal account; verify ad approval status without LegitScript certification.
[Philip D'Agostino] Send Instagram Screenshot: Send WhatsApp screenshot of the Valhalla Vitality Instagram page to Eugene.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Investigate Instagram Block: Contact Jasmine and Olesia regarding the Valhalla Vitality Instagram block preventing viewing.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Follow Up RXSC Payment: Send follow-up email to Peaks Creative regarding overdue payment for website work; include Philip and Sam.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Plan Evaluation: Hold a training meeting on Thursday; discuss AI requirements for the next 6 months with the team.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Improve Dashboard: Improve and tweak the built finance dashboard for better functionality.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Develop Plan: Prepare the peptides plan before the meeting tomorrow.

1tVxCwUlX0KkoKtbIKxEbDvsHFv0aE0ROJYBYbU2.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1tVxCwUlX0KkoKtbIKxEbDvsHFv0aE0ROJYBYbU2.summary:
Monitoring system and S3 storage changes were completed, prompting discussions on two-step registration, data migration priorities, and critical hotfixes.

Monitoring, Storage, and Registration
Monitoring system changes and S3 storage setup for Medusa were completed and deployed. Two-step registration fields matching Valhalla were implemented, and the team suggested implementing similar data synchronization visualization within the Medusa dashboard.

Critical Hotfixes and Priorities
The team directed a hotfix for the LifeFile tracking information to take precedence over fixing prescription errors, as receiving tracking data is the primary value of the API integration. Illia Tutko was directed to fix a critical issue where the clinical difference statement field was locked, preventing necessary text entry for script processing.

Data Migration and External Delays
Data migration was designated as the highest priority to unblock other tasks and prevent critical customer billing errors. Concern was raised regarding the external peak team causing delays by taking a week to address a 30-minute question, and Paul Edward Borja will follow up with the web team.
nextSteps: [Danylo Boiko] Move Database: Move Impetus staging database to the new environment for testing during non-working hours.
[Danylo Boiko] Inform Team: Notify Artem about the available S3 bucket storage for holding medical intake photo IDs and documents.
[The group] Document Process: Document the procedure for supporting new client sandbox creation managed by Illia.
[Gregory Popov] Review Migration: Coordinate with Elguja to review running the data migration and HubSpot integration as is.
[Gregory Popov] Document E-commerce: Document existing e-commerce processes, focusing on configurations and hand-over materials.
[Gregory Popov] Discuss Questions: Reach out to Paul Edward Borja later to discuss outstanding questions and pending topics.
[Paul Edward Borja] Contact Web Team: Send a message to the web development team regarding outstanding integration items following the meeting.
[Paul Edward Borja] Follow up: Send a follow-up chat message to the Peak team concerning the week-old address queries.

1tF-l7YQEsbxYBlyqbFFbkR_JsYdnubyrfGEppbH.title: Sprint Planning
1tF-l7YQEsbxYBlyqbFFbkR_JsYdnubyrfGEppbH.summary:
RX database and Medusa migrations were finished with verification and testing planned, while e-commerce and Impetus RX platform development continued with focus on high-priority tickets.

RX and Medusa Migration
The RX database and Medusa migration to Amazon were completed, but the Medusa migration logs showed warnings and potential missing records that require review. Rizzi Ann Gorospe was tasked with double-checking the staging database for proper data movement.

E-commerce and Swagger Updates
Work continued on e-commerce and Swagger endpoints, fixing issues with order retrieval and missing images, which currently retrieves data locally pending configuration for S3 storage. The team is prioritizing customer functionality and implementing the loyalty plugin features like VIP scores and points.

Impetus RX Platform Development
Illia Tutko completed production tickets related to memo fields, signatures, and removing the CDS field on prescriptions, and was assigned to investigate the critical pending status issue VV1349 impacting production orders for Hallandale. The team also determined they have enough tickets for the patient migration process to last at least 2 weeks.
nextSteps: [Danylo Boiko] Send Invite: Send Rizzi Ann Gorospe an invite to access Medusa staging.
[Gregory Popov] Review Documentation: Examine required documentation for handover next week.
[Gregory Popov] Catch Up: Catch up with Konrad regarding upcoming tasks.
[Gregory Popov] Configure Production: Set up the production instance and migrate customers for subscription handling.
[Rochelle Blaza] Contact Pix Team: Call Johan and attempt to contact Chris and Mario about the Pix Curative project delays.
[Gregory Popov] Investigate Products: Look into product tires and product visibility within WordPress.
[Rochelle Blaza] Get Test Account: Ask if a live account with a credit card is available for subscription testing.
[Gregory Popov] Research Subscriptions: Research the feasibility of moving subscriptions to authorize.net.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Push Guidelines: Push the pharmacy dosing guidelines update to staging for testing.
[Gregory Popov] Review PR: Review the Pull Request for pharmacy dosing guidelines.
[Illia Tutko] Investigate Errors: Investigate Grafana logs for the pending status issue and send comments on the ticket.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Send Order IDs: Send Illia the order IDs for Hallandale currently showing a pending status.

15n8gz74Wj4G6EmEyfyWVYqDg1arkiwdIryEopE2.title: Daily Standup Meeting
15n8gz74Wj4G6EmEyfyWVYqDg1arkiwdIryEopE2.summary:
Project updates addressed patient data export, Sage order migration, and Curative endpoint changes, with a focus on preparing front-end data for the user dashboard.

Data Export and Workflow Deployment
All patient data was successfully exported, organized, and normalized from PracticeSync with errors resolved. The SQL scripts used for data extraction must be shared via a ticket, and remaining workflows need deployment to production.

Sage Order Migration Status
Migration of orders still going to Sage is on hold, requiring discussion to determine the status. A test must be run to determine how many patients are still linked to Sage before attempting any further movement.

Front-End Data Preparation and Syncing
The Peaks Curative team confirmed they will update the pigs endpoint, requiring a wait for the updated information. Front-end data syncing, including subscriptions and payment methods, is being implemented to provide the necessary swagger endpoints for the user dashboard.
nextSteps: [Nadjib Boumekhiet] Update Ticket: Update the ticket; attach the SQL script used for pulling patient data.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Contact Konrad: Contact Konrad to discuss the unresolved issue regarding orders for Sage.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Run Script: Run the script to check the number of patients still linked to Sage.
[Rochelle Blaza] Upload Spreadsheet: Upload the Excel spreadsheet mentioned by Elguja into the relevant ticket.
[Illia Tutko] Check Pending: Look into the pending ticket after completing current work tasks.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Coordinate Farhan: Talk to Farhan and update the status of his tasks on the board.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Modify Prompt: Look into the prescription module prompt; remove United Pharmacy text.
[Rochelle Blaza] Follow Up Curative: Follow up with the Peaks Curative team to ensure they do not postpone the endpoint update.
[Elguja Nemsadze] Sync Migration: Sync today to analyze migration progress; discuss finalized endpoints and payment module requirements.
[Rochelle Blaza] Inform Artem’s Team: Update the Artem’s team about their dependency on the swagger API endpoints (ticket 1255).
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Contact Hallandale: Ask Kyle to call Hallandale pharmacy contact; seek approval to configure the LifeFile web hook.

1brRcNAhHfBJy57fEJHPtwK1ZstYkme2Fdnh8iE5.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
1brRcNAhHfBJy57fEJHPtwK1ZstYkme2Fdnh8iE5.summary:
Funding expectation, technical migration, and performance realignment dominated meeting discussions.

Funding and Website Migration
Funding is expected within a 4-month timeline, with an anticipated initial allocation of $500,000 for marketing and the website launch within 30 days of closing. The team plans to migrate the slow Ironsail website to Next.js to facilitate necessary AI-related content and product updates, running the new site in parallel to minimize risk.

CPA Realignment and Performance
A rough month due to high Cost Per Acquisition and poor influencer performance necessitated manual budget realignments, resulting in positive performance and projected 15% growth this week. The influencer and affiliate marketing channels are costly and yielding poor results, while Google Ads are performing extremely well with low sign-up costs.

Product Focus and Developer Strategy
Opportunities were identified for cost-effective peptide campaigns, with PMAX validating profitable sign-up conversions for products like PT141 and Modafinil at $5 to $30. Developers are being evaluated for their adoption of AI, with a new focus on AI-first development and training for Impetus One production, as non-adopting developers are expected to become obsolete.
nextSteps: [Eugene Ugolkov] Find Password: Locate the new password changed after Mola was fired.
[Philip D'Agostino] Inform Mazen: Tell Mazen about the high cost and low performance of affiliate marketing channels.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Launch Campaigns: Launch small test campaigns ($10-$30 daily) for cheap conversion search terms (e.g., modafinil, provigil).
[Eugene Ugolkov] Connect Ahrefs: Start connecting Ahrefs APIs for Cattix to obtain additional data.
[Philip D'Agostino] Contact Adan: Tell Adan to get in touch with Eugene regarding the business deal.

1LVKfSajNtDJxnW5GORYfDO6Yci7rEe7T5rLWwZP.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1LVKfSajNtDJxnW5GORYfDO6Yci7rEe7T5rLWwZP.summary:
Swagger endpoint sharing via ticket, for frontend pickup, with loyalty program and bonus research planned, followed by discussions on product visibility, historical subscriptions, saved payments, and workflow migrations to N8N.

Swagger Endpoint Staging Build
The main Eclipse flow authentication, ordering, pre-orders history, and card creation was manually tested and is ready for frontend work once the staging build is complete. The decision was made to keep the complex blueprint workflow in Zapier and focus testing on the World Vitality Wellness Blueprint workflow in Medusa staging.

Loyalty Program and Visibility
Loyalty program and bonuses will be picked up with research planned for existing WooCommerce plugins to replicate Medusa functionality. Product visibility requires further collaboration with Artem's team beyond the current Swagger data migration implementation.

HubSpot API and Workflow Migration
HubSpot API integration into Medusa is underway, and multiple order issues were resolved. The dependency of the wellness blueprint workflow on the medical intake form from Artem's team was discussed, along with a plan to confirm the webhook trigger for blueprint generation.
nextSteps: [Elguja Nemsadze] Loyalty Program: Review WooCommerce plugin, replicate same functionality for Medusa.
[Rochelle Blaza] Assign Tickets: Tag or assign loyalty program user stories to Elguja.
[Rochelle Blaza] Inform Artem: Notify Artem about the staging build being ready today.
[Gregory Popov] Write API: Develop API for the order history functionality.
[Gregory Popov] Consult Konrad: Discuss historical subscriptions with Konrad.
[Gregory Popov] Provide Update: Notify Rochelle after consulting with Konrad and Guja.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet, Farhan Sheikh] Build Workflow: Deploy existing blueprint workflow into N8N; build second workflow for Medusa.
[Rochelle Blaza] Talk Philip: Discuss strategy regarding complex workflow migration from Zapier to N8N with Philip.
[Illia Tutko] Deploy Fix: Send display prescription rejection fixes to code review, then release to staging.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Check Portal: Verify if pending orders are visible in Hallandale portal production.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Report Status: Update the team once a new report is received regarding the VV1261 recurring issue.
[Rochelle Blaza, Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Assign Ticket: Assign VV1261 ticket for investigation to Nadjib.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Investigate Issue: Investigate recurring VV1261 issue in Impetus RX related to duplicate orders.

1z35ItSZf_j3Pt5hvJXwmlj0ZsuPCN3EEkhWUfaE.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1z35ItSZf_j3Pt5hvJXwmlj0ZsuPCN3EEkhWUfaE.summary:
Backend team updates covered prescription ticket resolution and migration script adjustments with front-end testing blocked pending credential sharing.

Prescription Error Display Updates
Work is progressing on 2 similar prescription error tickets, and anticipated completion is today. It was decided that Hallandale error displays must be prioritized because that pharmacy is currently live.

Migration Script Status
The Medusa migration script terminated early, and the truncating functionality is not working as expected. Adjustments are being made to the migration script to run continuously and to allow smaller data imports for initial testing.

Product Visibility Logic Review
Investigation is required into why the portal is incorrectly showing a pending status for successfully received orders. The team confirmed they are testing the product visibility plugin against the simplified logic restricting products based on user registration.
nextSteps: [Illia Tutko] Investigate Pending: Investigate the root cause for prescription pending status failing to reflect correctly. Resolve the issue causing duplicate orders to be sent.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Help Adan: Help Adan with the principle of the day task.
[Nadjib Boumekhiet] Resolve Support: Resolve remaining support tickets located in his board.
[Gregory Popov] Adjust Migration: Adjust the migration script to sync up delta instead of running once. Configure the script to run in the background.
[Gregory Popov] Create Ticket: Create a new ticket for the migration script adjustment task.
[Elguja Nemsadze] Send Parameters: Generate and send migration script parameters to Danylo. Allow running the script for a small chunk of users (e.g., 100 users).
[Elguja Nemsadze] Test API Key: Test the publishable API key for Medusa storefront integration. Perform testing once initial migrated users are available.
[Elguja Nemsadze, Danylo Boiko] Share Credentials: Share necessary swagger and admin credentials with the front end team today. Ensure test users are successfully migrated before sharing.
[Elguja Nemsadze] Check Product Visibility: Work on product visibility logic validation. Check Phillips existing plugin code, validating visitor, user, and patient functionality works as expected.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Deploy Guidelines: Push the dosing guidelines update to the staging environment for testing.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Work Pharmacy Tickets: Move to working on tickets 1365 and 1366. Update Pharmacy Marketplace query logic.
[Farhan Sheikh] Test Spot DX: Test the Spot DX integration with Medusa after the call concludes.
[Farhan Sheikh] Cancel Order: Cancel the test order from the dashboard. Execute cancellation after successful integration testing is complete.

1wbYNOVgtRK8oyTztbIHGz-ykg_MS9P_Wm2zyFi9.title: Ironsail x Webugol Internal Marketing
1wbYNOVgtRK8oyTztbIHGz-ykg_MS9P_Wm2zyFi9.summary:
AI development discussion explored the rapid changes in AI tools and the necessity of automated systems for staying competitive, concluding with the decision to acquire the AI Ultra subscription for enhanced capabilities.

Initial AI Tool Exploration
The discussion shifted to alternative AI tools after a temporary block on Claude, leading to an evaluation of Codex and Antigravity for code generation. Initial comparisons found Codex 5.4 extremely cheap compared to Claude, prompting a strategy to use Gemini 3.1 Pro within Anti-gravity to plan work, with Codex handling implementation.

Advanced AI Workflow Strategy
The need for a robust AI workflow was solidified by the decision to acquire the AI Ultra subscription, granting expanded quotas for Gemini and other models like Claude and GPT OSS. It was determined that the optimal strategy involves centralizing project knowledge via the Antigravity agent manager and utilizing a project-specific Claude MD file on GitHub for team consistency and continuous integration.

Future AI Infrastructure Planning
The rapid pace of AI innovation requires building robust infrastructure, including automated systems like Robothor to track and integrate new features without manual work. Future plans include self-funding the project and establishing local, powerful compute infrastructure, such as converting a Florida studio apartment into a data center with solar power, to reduce reliance on Claude services.
nextSteps: [Eugene Ugolkov] Install Extension: Install the Claude code extension for anti-gravity.
[Philip D'Agostino] Download VS Code: Download and use the Claude extension for VS Code.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Continue Development: Continue building the SEO blog generator project using Codex.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Upgrade Subscription: Switch to the AI Ultra subscription temporarily while Claude access is unavailable.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Prepare Proposal: Use Claude to build a detailed proposal including numbers and structure for Anthony.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Send Gradation: Send the pricing gradation shell to Anthony explaining cost scaling.
[Philip D'Agostino] Implement Sharing: Start implementing shared AI accounts among team members.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Contact Mazen: Reach out to Mazen regarding the Uber Doc project.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Install Robothor: Install the Robothor agent system. Try the system to understand its functionality and onboarding.
[Philip D'Agostino] Share Server Plan: Notify Eugene Ugolkov about plans to build a local solar-powered server or data center. Eugene will order similar equipment for personal deployment.
[Philip D'Agostino] Acquire GPU: Purchase at least 1 Nvidia GPU, possibly the Reuben Vera model, for local AI processing capabilities.
